title: The File of Fatima - Episode 1: Illuminations Before Starting
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 1

In the inaugural episode of the File of Fatima, Sheikh al-Ghizzi outlines his investigative methodology, rejecting the manipulated game of chains of narration (Asanid) in favor of forensic analysis of historical contexts (Qara'in). He establishes a profound Quranic principle of complicity, demonstrating that those who validate the oppressors of the **Ahlulbayt** bear the guilt of their crimes, with the blood of Fatima smeared on their clothes until the Day of Resurrection.

Drawing from highly sensitive and often censored texts across both Sunni and Shia traditions—including Sahih Muslim, Ibn Qutaybah's Al-Imamah wa al-Siyasah, al-Juwayni's Fara'id al-Simtayn, a suppressed letter in **Bihar al-Anwar**, and the foundational **Kamil al-Ziyarat**—the Sheikh exposes a systematic campaign of burying the truth. He conclusively proves through these diverse historical fragments that the Prophet's daughter did not merely die of grief, but was brutally oppressed, usurped, and murdered following a violent assault on her home, setting the stage for a detailed examination of her martyrdom.
Segment 1: Illuminations Before Embarking

Sheikh al-Ghizzi introduces the "File of Fatima" series, setting three foundational premises: first, his intent is not to provoke but to present buried truths from both Shia and Sunni historical texts; second, he highlights the paradox of the Muslim Ummah abandoning the Prophet's only surviving child despite the Quranic command to love his near kin; and third, he asserts that true Shiism is defined solely by the Infallible Ahlulbayt, not by any fallible scholar or cleric.

Peace be upon Fatima, the Chosen (al-Mustafa), and Al-Murtada her husband, and the Infallibles from her children. Peace be upon the Fatimids in every time and place, her offspring, her Shia, and her lovers, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings.

The Fatimi File, Episode One: Illuminations Before Embarking. Points I loved to point out before I take up the papers of this file. The first point: It was my desire that the episodes of this program be long, so that I could research in them every small and big detail relating to this topic from near or far. But it occurred to my mind that this program should be translated into the most important languages spoken by the majority of the Shia of the Ahlulbayt in the world... I will try to be brief, but a brevity that does not compromise and does not subtract from the important demands around which the papers of this file revolve.

As for the second point: The speech in these episodes is not a provocation to anyone, nor a love for incitement or for noise. It is a clarification of truths. These truths are present in the books of the Shia of Fatima, and in the books of others! **The Fatimi File: papers from the bellies of the books of hadith and the books of history.** The whip of the oppressors has fallen upon it, or the forgery of the pens of historians and hadith narrators from the worshippers of dirhams and from those who eat the crumbs of the tables of sultans.

The atrocity of the crime... and it was registered against an unknown perpetrator! Thus history judged the oppression of Fatima: either denial, or casting doubt, or forgery, or distortion. So that the Fatimi File would be absent in the world of oblivion, and placed in the archive of heedlessness, where the dust of disloyalty to Muhammad, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, accumulates upon it.
When the Seal of the Prophets, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, used to stand in his noble mosque while preaching among his companions and saying:
May Allah curse whoever deprives the hired worker of his wage... And I am your hired worker! 'Say, I do not ask of you for it any reward except love for the near of kin.'

And I ask myself before I ask others: What person in this world is the closest to the Messenger of Allah? Is there anyone closer in kinship and stronger in blood-tie and connection than Fatima? Fatima is the daughter of Muhammad. And Muhammad departed from the world and he had no one in this world—neither a father, nor a mother, nor a child—except Fatima! The speech here is about Fatima. About the oppression of Fatima, about the tragedy of the daughter of Muhammad.

The third point: It is my conviction that I clarify, it is not a proof against anyone, and I do not want to impose my opinion on anyone, nor do I represent any entity among the entities. **Shiism (al-Tashayyu') represents nothing except the Ahlulbayt.** A perfect religion cannot be represented by the deficient (al-Naqis), no matter what he reaches of virtue, righteousness, knowledge, and jurisprudence. The authoritative proof of the people of knowledge is only an incidental proof and a relative proof, to the extent of their closeness to the Ahlulbayt. As it came in the noble signed decree (Tawqi'):
And as for the occurring events, return in them to the narrators of our hadith, for they are my proof over you, and I am the proof of Allah over them.

There is no one on earth who represents Shiism! And if there are found among the scholars, the religious authorities, the jurists, and the hadith scholars, then they represent that matter a relative representation. The complete, absolute authoritative proof is for Muhammad and the family of Muhammad.
Segment 2: Partners in the Crime - The Quranic Principle of Complicity

Sheikh al-Ghizzi answers the pivotal question: "Why open the File of Fatima?" He derives the answer directly from the Quran. Citing verses from Surah Al-Baqarah and Surah Al Imran, he demonstrates how Allah held the Jews of Medina responsible for killing the prophets, even though those murders occurred 500 years before they were born. Imam al-Sadiq explains that because the Jews of Medina loved and approved of their ancestors' actions, Allah deemed them direct accomplices. The Sheikh applies this Quranic principle to the Ummah: anyone who defends, loves, or approves of the killers of the Ahlulbayt is an active partner in their murder.

I do not want to wander far in these points. And I ask a question: Why do I open this file? Why the speech in the lines of the pages of this file? The answer comes from the orchards of the Book and the Progeny. In the Noble Book, here we go to Surah Al-Baqarah. And in the 91st verse:
Say, 'Then why did you kill the prophets of Allah before, if you are [indeed] believers?'

Did the Jews of Medina kill any prophet from the prophets? What do our Imams, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon them all, say? And this is the first volume of **Tafsir al-Burhan** by Sayyid Hashim al-Bahrani. The narration is from our **Imam al-Sadiq**, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. The Imam says:

This section of the Quran was only revealed regarding a group of Jews who were in the time of Muhammad. They did not kill the prophets of Allah with their hands, nor were they in their time. Rather, their early ancestors who were before them killed them... So Allah made them of them, and added to them the action of their early ancestors because they followed them and took them as masters.

And we do not go far, in Surah Al Imran. In the 183rd verse: "Say, 'There have already come to you messengers before me with clear proofs... So why did you kill them, if you should be truthful?'" The address is also to the Jews who were in the time of Muhammad.

The narration in the noble **al-Kafi**. And this is the second volume of the volumes of the noble **al-Kafi**. The narration is from our **Imam al-Sadiq**, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. The Imam says:

May Allah curse the Qadariyyah. May Allah curse the Kharijites. May Allah curse the Murji'ah. May Allah curse the Murji'ah... Indeed these say that our killers are believers! So our blood is smeared on their clothes until the Day of Resurrection!

What do these Murji'ah say? That the killers of the Ahlulbayt, Yazid ibn Muawiyah, is a believer, and his caliphate is legitimate! So we leave the talk about Fatima! Which clothes are smeared with the blood of Fatima and the family of Fatima? "That our killers are believers," these Murji'ah say thus, "So our blood is smeared on their clothes until the Day of Resurrection."
Then after that, the Imam points to the verse. The Imam says:
There was between the killers and the addressed five hundred years, so Allah bound them to the killing because of their being pleased with what they did!

The exact same thing, the Murji'ah say "our killers are believers." The killers of the Ahlulbayt are believers. The blood is there; the blood of the Ahlulbayt is smeared on their clothes until the Day of Resurrection. And that is not strange. The narrations state that the murdered one comes on the Day of Resurrection having been killed by one man, but the killers are gathered in dozens. Why? Because anyone who heard of the killing and rejoiced in it, he is a partner in his killing!
Segment 3: The Principle of Complicity and the Manifesto of the Mahdi

Sheikh al-Ghizzi deepens the principle of complicity by quoting Imam al-Baqir from Sheikh al-Saduq's 'Iqab al-A'mal. The Imam states that anyone who remains willfully ignorant of the Ahlulbayt's oppression is a partner to their oppressors. The Sheikh then recites the heartbreaking manifesto of Imam al-Mahdi, delivered by Al-Nafs al-Zakiyyah before his reappearance, which cries out for help against the continuous oppression of the Ahlulbayt since the Prophet's death.

A clear and explicit narration narrated by our Sheikh al-Saduq in his book **'Iqab al-A'mal**. The narration is from Jabir al-Ju'fi, from our **Imam al-Baqir**, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. What does our Imam al-Baqir say?

Whoever does not know the evil of what was brought upon us of our oppression, the taking away of our right, and what we were afflicted with, then he is a partner to whoever brought it upon us in what we were subjected to!

An issue of extreme importance! This is the speech of the Infallible Imam. The talk here is about a nation that claims that it is the nation of Muhammad, a nation that heard of the Ahlulbayt. Meaning the one who does not know the extent of the oppression that befell the Ahlulbayt—while it is possible for him to know—he is a partner to the one who oppressed the Ahlulbayt! This word requires the believing human to stop at it for a long time, to reflect upon it.

Our Imam of the Time, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. This is the 52nd volume of **Bihar al-Anwar**. And these are lines from the manifesto that our Imam of the Time sends with the Pure Soul (al-Nafs al-Zakiyyah). The messenger of the Proof says to the people of Mecca:

We are the Household of Mercy, and the mine of the Message and the Caliphate. And we are the progeny of Muhammad and the lineage of the prophets. And indeed we have been oppressed, and persecuted, and conquered, and our right was extorted from us since our Prophet was taken until this day of ours! So we seek your help, so help us!

This is the manifesto of the Imam of our time! And the broadest title, the greatest title in this oppression is the oppression of the mother of al-Hassan and al-Hussein: the Fatimi Oppression. This is a brief answer to a question I posed: why do we open the Fatimi File? The answer is in these luminous words.

As for how we deal with the papers of the Fatimi file. I do not want to research here upon the method of the historians or the hadith scholars, so I get lost in this game! In the game of sources and chains of narration which was fabricated by those who fabricated it! We are searching for this crime just as investigators search for crimes! We search for contexts (Qara'in). And from the known rules recognized in criminology: there is no such thing as a perfect crime. And from the rules also: that the criminal circles the location of his crime.
Segment 4: The Game of Chains of Narration and the Deception of al-Bukhari

Sheikh al-Ghizzi demonstrates the "game of chains of narration" by comparing the exact same hadith in Sahih Muslim and Sahih al-Bukhari. In Sahih Muslim, Umar explicitly admits that Imam Ali and al-Abbas viewed Abu Bakr and Umar as "liars, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest." However, Sahih al-Bukhari completely censors these specific words while keeping the rest of the hadith and the exact same chain of transmission.

This is Sahih al-Bukhari. And this is Sahih Muslim, and they are the most trusted books among the people [the Sunnis]. The narration in Sahih Muslim talks about a dialogue between al-Abbas the uncle of the Prophet, and the Master of Legatees [Ali], and Umar ibn al-Khattab. Umar says, addressing al-Abbas and Ali:

So Abu Bakr said: The Messenger of Allah said: We do not leave inheritance; what we leave is charity. So you both viewed him [Abu Bakr] as a liar, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest! And Allah knows that he is truthful, pious, rightly-guided, and a follower of the truth.
Then Umar continues:
Then Abu Bakr died, and I became the successor of the Messenger of Allah and the successor of Abu Bakr. So you both viewed me as a liar, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest!

This narration is an authentic narration in Sahih Muslim, and the one who transmits it is Umar. So Ali viewed Abu Bakr according to this narration as a liar, sinful, treacherous, and dishonest! And he viewed Umar likewise.

If you went to Sahih al-Bukhari... Al-Bukhari transmitted the exact same hadith, from the exact same narrator, from Malik ibn Aws, with the exact same chain of narration, with the exact same text, **except that he removed from the hadith these words which I pointed to!** Which are: the opinion of Ali and al-Abbas regarding Abu Bakr and Umar!

This is the "most authentic" of hadith books! Is this not deception (Tadlis)? Hiding of facts! So what is the use of the issue of chains of narration, and what is the use of the issue of sources if the issue is like this?! Then this is a game! It is said about al-Bukhari that he had 600,000 hadiths! If he had 600,000 hadiths and he only chose 2761, then how many truths disappeared in those hadiths?!

So if it was not a deception from al-Bukhari, and we said that the narrations reached al-Bukhari in this form, while they are present in other sources in another form, what does this mean? It means that relying on chains of narration alone, even with good intention, does not lead us to the truths! It leads us to deceived reports.
Segment 5: Ibn Qutaybah and the Confrontation at Fatima's Door

Sheikh al-Ghizzi turns to the renowned Sunni historian Ibn Qutaybah al-Dinawari's book, Al-Imamah wa al-Siyasah. He reads a harrowing account of Umar's mob arriving at Fatima's door, her agonizing cry to the Prophet, and the explicit threat to behead Imam Ali. The Sheikh highlights the profound theological implications of Fatima refusing to return the obligatory Islamic greeting (Salam) to Abu Bakr and Umar.

And therefore, when the talk about the oppression of Fatima comes in the books of the people, they hasten to deny it! For example: this book is Al-Imamah wa al-Siyasah by Ibn Qutaybah al-Dinawari. And he is from the scholars of the people, and from their hadith narrators, and from their historians. This book is explicit and clear regarding the oppression of Fatima. Page 22, page 23:

Then Umar stood up, and a group walked with him until they came to the door of Fatima. So they knocked on the door. When she heard their voices, she called out at the top of her voice: 'O Father! O Messenger of Allah! What have we met after you from Ibn al-Khattab and Ibn Abi Quhafah (Abu Bakr)!'
He continues:

But Umar remained, and with him a group. So they brought Ali out and took him to Abu Bakr. They said to him: 'Pledge allegiance.' He said: 'If I do not do it, then what will you do?' They said: 'Then, by Allah besides whom there is no god, we will strike your neck!'
Ibn Qutaybah also narrates:

So Umar said to Abu Bakr: 'Let us go to Fatima, for we have angered her.' So they both set out together, and they sought permission to enter upon Fatima, but she did not give them permission. So they came to Ali and spoke to him, so he entered them upon her. When they sat near her, she turned her face to the wall. They greeted her with Salam, but she did not return the Salam to them.

Returning the Salam to a Muslim is obligatory! And Fatima is infallible. Why did she not greet them? Because returning the Salam is obligatory upon the Muslim, even if he is in disagreement with him. She did not return the Salam to them. Then Ibn Qutaybah writes:

She said: 'I implore you by Allah, did you not hear the Messenger of Allah saying: The pleasure of Fatima is from my pleasure, and the anger of Fatima is from my anger...?' They said: 'Yes, we heard it from the Messenger of Allah.' She said: 'Then I make Allah and His angels witness that you both have angered me and did not please me! And if I meet the Prophet, I will surely complain to him about you both!'

This is the speech of Ibn Qutaybah. They denied it, and we do not care about their denial as I clarified a short while ago. This is a game: the game of researching in sources and chains of narration. This is a source from their sources, clear and explicit.
Segment 6: The Explicit Prophecy of the Murder of Fatima

Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents another profound piece of forensic evidence from the Sunni scholar al-Juwayni in his book Fara'id al-Simtayn. The Sheikh reads a devastating hadith narrated by Ibn Abbas, where the Prophet prophesies the exact calamities that will befall Fatima, and explicitly curses her oppressors and the one who strikes her.

The other source... They said that this book was falsely attributed to Ibn Qutaybah al-Dinawari. As for this second book, they said its author is a Shiite, that he had become a Rafidhi! This is Fara'id al-Simtayn by the hadith scholar al-Hamawini or al-Juwayni al-Khurasani.
The narration with his chain of transmission, he narrates it from Sa'id ibn Jubayr from Ibn Abbas. The Prophet began to speak about the oppression of his Ahlulbayt, until he said:

It is as if I am with her, and humiliation has entered her house, her sanctity has been violated, her right has been usurped, her inheritance has been denied, her side has been broken, and her fetus has been miscarried! And she is calling out: 'O Muhammadah!' but she is not answered... And she will be the first to join me from my Ahlulbayt. So she will come to me sorrowful, distressed, oppressed, usurped, and murdered (Maqtulah)!

A clear, explicit statement! The book is from the books of the people (the Sunnis). The hadith is not from the Shia, it is from Sa'id ibn Jubayr from Ibn Abbas. The Messenger of Allah says, at that moment what does he say?
O Allah, curse whoever oppressed her! And punish whoever usurped from her, and humiliate whoever humiliated her! And abide forever in Your Fire whoever struck her side until she miscarried her child!
And Sahih al-Bukhari is not far from these atmospheres. In Sahih al-Bukhari, page 1190, Hadith 6725 and 6726:

So Abu Bakr said to them: I heard the Messenger of Allah... saying: We do not leave inheritance; what we leave is charity... He said: So Fatima abandoned him, and she did not speak to him until she died!

We do not expect from al-Bukhari—who deleted those words and deleted many words—to bring us the full story as al-Juwayni mentioned it. We search for contexts here and there. These contexts, when gathered, the picture will appear clear and it will manifest evident and plain.
Segment 7: The Suppressed Document - Umar's Letter to Muawiyah

Sheikh al-Ghizzi unveils a chilling and historically suppressed document found in the 30th volume of Bihar al-Anwar. The document is a long letter written by Umar ibn al-Khattab to Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan, extracted from the lost second volume of Dala'il al-Imamah. In this letter, Umar explicitly confesses to the horrifying details of the attack on Fatima's house.

There is a document present, but no one mentions it. This is the thirtieth volume of **Bihar al-Anwar**. From the volumes that were prevented from being printed! And the one who prevented its printing was one of our Maraji' (religious authorities). Everyone knows that **Bihar al-Anwar** was printed without the volumes from the twenty-ninth volume to the thirty-fourth volume.

This is a very important document, page 210, Hadith number 151. **Sheikh al-Majlisi** says: "Some of the virtuous scholars in Mecca... informed me that he extracted it from the second volume of the book **Dala'il al-Imamah**." **Dala'il al-Imamah** by the hadith scholar al-Tabari the Imami (Shia). This letter is a detailed letter from Umar ibn al-Khattab to Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan, telling him about the events that occurred. Among what he says, he says:

...and I took the whip and I struck! And I said to Khalid ibn al-Walid: 'You and our men, come forward in gathering the firewood!' So I said: 'I will surely set it on fire'—meaning set fire to the house. So she said—meaning Fatima—'O enemy of Allah, and enemy of His Messenger, and enemy of the Commander of the Believers!' And Fatima struck her hands on the door to prevent me from opening it. So I pushed it while it was difficult for her. So I struck her hands with the whip, and it pained her.
He continues his confession to Muawiyah:

But I remembered the grudges of Ali... So I kicked the door, while she had stuck her insides to the door to barricade it! I heard her... and she let out a cry that I thought had turned the top of Medina to its bottom! And she said: 'O Father! O Messenger of Allah! Thus is it done to your beloved and your daughter! Ah! Take me to you, for by Allah, the one in my insides has been killed, O Muhammad!'
And the final harrowing detail:

And I heard her in labor pains while she was leaning against the wall. So I pushed the door and entered. She came at me with a face whose sight I feared, so I slapped her a slap on her cheeks from outside the veil (Khimar), and her earring broke and scattered to the ground!

This text is part of a long, wide letter written by Umar ibn al-Khattab to Muawiyah, according to this narration which our **Sheikh al-Majlisi** narrated in **Bihar al-Anwar**. This is among the texts that were hidden! Among the texts that are not mentioned, not on the pulpits, nor are they mentioned on satellite channels, nor are they mentioned in public gatherings!
Segment 8: The Conclusive Proof from Kamil al-Ziyarat - Death by Beating

Sheikh al-Ghizzi concludes the first episode by addressing critics who accused him of fabricating the claim that Imam al-Sadiq stated Fatima died specifically from being beaten. To silence them, he reads an undeniable, explicit hadith from the foundational Shia text Kamil al-Ziyarat. The text explicitly states that Fatima will be beaten while pregnant, her home will be invaded, she will miscarry due to the strikes, and she will definitively "die from that beating."

As for **Kamil al-Ziyarat**... I spoke about how in the reports from our **Imam al-Sadiq**, peace be upon him, it states that Fatima, prayers of Allah be upon her, was beaten and died from the beating. And it reached me that some individuals denied this matter! And they said that I am the one who fabricated it, and that no such thing exists!

This is **Kamil al-Ziyarat**. And the narration here, the narration is narrated by our **Imam al-Sadiq** from the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family. The narration from Hammad ibn Uthman, from Abu Abdullah (**Imam al-Sadiq**), peace be upon him:

When the Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, was taken on the Night Journey (Mi'raj) to the heaven, it was said to him: 'Indeed, Allah the Blessed and Exalted will test you in three things...' And as for the third: 'What your Ahlulbayt will face after you of killing.'
The explicit proof follows:

And as for your daughter, she will be oppressed, deprived, her right which you designated for her will be usurped by force, and she will be beaten while she is pregnant! And her sanctuary and her home will be entered upon her without permission! Then humiliation and abasement will touch her, and she will find no protector. And she will miscarry what is in her womb from the beating! And she will die from that beating!

What is the difference between this narration and the narration of the opponents in Fara'id al-Simtayn which I read a short while ago: "So she will come to me sorrowful, distressed, oppressed, usurped, and murdered"? This narration [in **Kamil al-Ziyarat**] is from Hammad ibn Uthman from our **Imam al-Sadiq**, peace be upon him, from the Messenger of Allah. **Fatima died from the beating! Fatima was murdered, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon her.**

So Fatima dies from that beating. And the question here: Then how severe was that beating because of which she died?! How was Fatima beaten? And where was Fatima beaten?! These details will come to us, God willing the Exalted, in the next episode.

title: The File of Fatima - Episode 2: The Game of Sources and Chains of Narrators
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 2

In this episode, Sheikh al-Ghizzi addresses the methodological "gatekeeping" used by traditional scholastic establishments to dismiss the details of Lady Fatima’s oppression. He introduces the concept of **"The Game of Sources,"** arguing that while verifying narrations is logical, modern "Rijal" sciences are often used as a tool of censorship. He establishes the **Book of Sulaym ibn Qays** as the foundational document of Shi'a history and provides a harrowing eye-witness account of the physical assault on Lady Fatima's house. The episode concludes by contrasting the intellectual and spiritual deficiency of the early Caliphs (as found in Sunni Sahihs) with the absolute divine decree that the Prophet’s pleasure is tied solely to the pleasure of Fatima.
Segment 1: Recap and the Search for Sincerity

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens with spiritual salutations and sets the schedule for the series. He previews the next episode on the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays, calling it the most vital document concerning the "Great Fitna." He clarifies that while verifying sources is a logical necessity, it is often practiced as a "game" to obscure the truth. He cites Al-Kafi, emphasizing that the Infallibles instructed the Shi'a to take religion from "trustworthy and secure" narrators closely linked to their secrets.

Peace be upon Fatima the chosen one and al-Murtada, her husband and the Infallibles from her children and her sons. Peace be upon the Fatimids in every time and place from her descendants, her Shi'a, and her lovers, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings. **The File of Fatima - Episode 2.**

I want to clarify an important matter in this episode. Tomorrow, the talk will be about the **most important document** regarding the oppression of the daughter of the Messenger of Allah: **The Book of Sulaym ibn Qays**. The Book of Sulaym ibn Qays is among the most vital documents and sources that speak about the history of the **"Great Fitna"** that occurred after the departure of the Messenger of Allah (blessings of Allah be upon him and his family).

So, tomorrow the talk is about Sulaym ibn Qays and the texts mentioned in this noble book regarding the oppression of the mother of al-Hassan and al-Hussain. The talk tomorrow is about **how Fatima was struck, and how many times she was struck**. As for today's talk in this second episode, it is a clarification and confirmation of what passed in the first episode. I want to complete the discussion on the requirements mentioned previously so that I do not return to them in future episodes.

Among the requirements and topics I referred to and spoke about in the previous episode is what I called **"The Game of Sources and Chains of Narrators"** (Ulubat al-Masadir wa al-Asanid). I want to clarify this matter more deeply. When I said that relying on sources and chains is a "game" and that I will not [exclusively] rely on them in the File of Fatima, this talk is **not a negation** of the importance of sources and chains. Never.

Confirming the correctness and strength of sources and the reliability of chains is a **natural, human, and logical issue** before it even enters the discussions of science or legal matters. The human nature (fitra) pushes one to confirm sources. But there is a difference between a matter being correct and logical in theory, and the **practical reality**. When I said this is a "game," I was referring to the practical reality as it exists today.

**Illumination 1:** I will deal with texts from the Hadith of the Ahl al-Bayt. The narration is from the noble **Al-Kafi** by our Sheikh al-Kulayni. Ahmed ibn Ishaq narrates from Abu al-Hassan (peace be upon him)—referring to our Imam al-Hadi. Ahmed ibn Ishaq was among the greatest companions of the Imams and one of their agents. Ahmed ibn Ishaq said to the Imam: "From whom should I deal, from whom should I take [knowledge], and whose word should I accept?" The Imam replied:

Al-Amri is my trustworthy one. Whatever he delivers to you from me, he delivers from me. And whatever he says to you from me, he says from me. So listen to him and obey, for he is the Trustworthy and Secure (al-Thiqah al-Ma'mun).
Segment 2: The Trap of Scholastic "Reliability"

Sheikh al-Ghizzi discusses the concept of "The Trustworthy and Secure." He cites a narration where Imam al-Askari confirms al-Amri and his son are trustworthy, establishing that obedience should be reserved for those the Imams vetted. He explains that while the Imams delegated authority to narrators, this is relative, not absolute. He warns that the "Game of Sources" in the modern Hawza has corrupted this, using "mathematical" evaluation to weaken authentic reports about the Ahl al-Bayt’s secrets.

The Imam said: "So listen to him and obey, for he is the Trustworthy and Secure." This means that hearing, obedience, and total acceptance are only for the **Trustworthy and Secure**. This is a natural, logical, and intuitive matter that requires no debate. Similarly, Ahmed ibn Ishaq asked Imam al-Askari (peace be upon him) the same question: "From whom should I take [knowledge]?" The Imam replied:
Al-Amri and his son are two trustworthy ones. Whatever they deliver to you from me, they deliver from me. So listen to them and obey them, for they are the Trustworthy and Secure ones.

The **Reality of Religion** is that we follow the one who is "Secure" in their transmission. But the **Reality of the World** in the scholastic establishment has turned this into a "Game." Look at the **Signed Decree** (al-Tawqi' al-Sharif) issued from the Sacred Side [Imam Mahdi], narrated in **Rijal al-Kashshi**. It states:
There is no excuse for any of our followers to doubt what our trustworthy ones narrate from us.

The Imam says there is **no excuse** for doubt. Why? Because the followers know that the Imams have entrusted their secrets to these specific people. The issue of "Reliability" (Wathaqa) and the correctness of the source is a foundational principle. However, the "vortex" of the Tusi Hawza has changed the meaning of this. They created **Ilm al-Rijal**, which is a "dry game." They look at a narrator and say, "He is weak," not because he lied about the Imam, but because he doesn't fit the "rules" they imported from the opponents!

They use these rules to strike down the Hadiths that mention the **"Secrets of the Progeny"** or the **"Details of the Oppression."** They tell the people: "This book is not authentic," or "This chain is broken," even when the content of the Hadith screams of the "Light of the Infallible." This is why I call it a **Game**. If the content of a narration is aligned with the Quran and the clear path of the Ahl al-Bayt, these "scholars" will still reject it if the chain doesn't satisfy their "mathematical" requirements. They have made the **Chain** an idol that is worshipped instead of the **Truth**.

The Imams want us to look at the **Source**. And the Source is the "Light" that the narrator carries. If the narrator is "Secure" (Ma'mun), he will not distort the message. But the modern establishment is not "Secure"; they are "Censors" for the Sultans and for their own social status. In the **File of Fatima**, we will not play this game. We will not allow a "Tusi rule" to dismiss the tragedy of Zahra. We will look at the **Consensus of the Hearts** and the **Internal Evidence** of the texts. If the "Secure Narrators" of the early generations recorded it, we accept it, even if a "modern Ayatollah" wants to weaken it to please the "Reality of the World."
Segment 3: The Authentication of the Heart

Sheikh al-Ghizzi deepens his critique of "The Game of Chains." He argues that the "Tusi Methodology" uses it to filter out narrations revealing "uncomfortable" truths. He discusses how early Shi'a scholars emphasized the reliability of the source person and purity of content over formalistic lists of names. He asserts the truth of Lady Fatima's oppression is so pervasive that trying to "weaken" individual reports is a futile attempt to obscure a radiant sun.

We must understand that there is a **General Atmosphere** that the Imams created for their Shi'a. They did not leave us to be toys in the hands of "chain experts" who would decide for us what is true and what is false based on their own biases. The Imams said: "Take what is agreed upon by your people and leave the rare." But what is "agreed upon"? The Tusi establishment says it is what **they** agree upon in their modern Hawzas. No! It is what was agreed upon by the **trustworthy companions** of the Imams who lived through the events.

When we look at the **Book of Sulaym ibn Qays**, they try to tell you: "It is a weak book because of Aban ibn Abi Ayyash." They use one name in a chain to destroy the **First Book of Shi'a History**. This is the "Game" I am talking about. I ask them: If the book is weak, why did the Imams praise it? Why did they say: "Whoever does not have the Book of Sulaym... has nothing of our matter"? They ignore the **Infallible Witness** to protect their "scholastic rules" which they borrowed from the enemies of the Ahl al-Bayt.

The "Game of Sources" is used as a **Censorship Tool**. If a report says that Lady Fatima was struck, they look for a way to "weaken" the narrator. But if a report supports the status quo or the "political unity," they accept it with very little scrutiny. The **Reality of Religion** is that the truth has its own light. When you read a report about the oppression of Zahra, and it matches the dozens of other reports from both Shi'a and Sunni sources, the "chain" becomes secondary. The **Content** validates itself through its consistency with the "Zahra’i Light."

They have turned **Ilm al-Rijal** into a **Barrier of Darkness**. They use it to prevent the Shi'a from reaching the true "File of Fatima." They want to keep the file "closed" or "doubtful" so they don't have to face the consequences of the truth. But the early Shi'a were not like this. They took the reports from the **Trustworthy and Secure** narrators because they recognized the "voice" of the Imam in those reports. They had an **Intellectual Sensitivity** that the modern Tusi scholar has lost due to centuries of Greek logic and Sunni jurisprudence.

We open this file based on the **Authenticity of the Heart** and the **Consensus of the Progeny**. We don't need a "permission" from the Hawza to believe in the broken rib of our Mother. The documents are in the history, they are in the hearts, and they are even hidden in the books of the oppressors. In the next segments, we will go deeper into the **Book of Sulaym** and show why it is the "Alphabet of Shi'ism." If you understand Sulaym, you understand the entire tragedy. And if you understand the tragedy, you will finally understand why the Imam of our Time is in Occultation. The file is not about the past; it is about the **Living Pain** of the present.
Segment 4: The Foundation of Shi'a History

Sheikh al-Ghizzi focuses on the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays, the "oldest and most important" book of Shi'a history. He cites al-Mas'udi, who stated this book is the foundational source for the Shi'a. He addresses the attacks on its authenticity, explaining that the "Game of Sources" casts doubt on it because it contains graphic accounts of the attack on Lady Fatima’s house. He asserts Imam al-Sadiq endorsed the book as the "Alphabet of the Shi'a."

Now we reach the **Heart of the File**. I am talking about the book that is considered the "Alpha" of Shi'a documentation: **The Book of Sulaym ibn Qays al-Hilali**. Who is Sulaym? He was a companion of the Commander of the Faithful (Ali), al-Hassan, al-Hussain, and Ali ibn al-Hussain. He was a man who lived through the "Great Fitna" and wrote down what his eyes saw and what his ears heard from the Infallibles themselves.
The historian **al-Mas'udi**—who is respected by both Shi'a and Sunnis—says in his book "Al-Tanbih wa al-Ishraf":
The oldest book known to the Shi'a, which they rely upon and refer to, is the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays.

This is the "First Record." It predates **Al-Kafi**, it predates the **Four Books**, it predates everything. So why is there a war against this book? Why do the "Tusi scholars" use their "Game of Chains" to say it is "subject to doubt"?

The answer is simple: Because this book leaves **no room for compromise**. It contains the papers that document the attack on the house, the gathering of the wood, the threat of fire, and the crushing of the "Leader of the Women" behind the door. If you accept Sulaym, you must accept that the "Caliphate" was established on a foundation of blood and fire. The **Reality of the World** cannot handle this truth. So, they target the **Narrator** (Aban ibn Abi Ayyash) to destroy the **Source**.
But let us see what the **Reality of Religion** says. Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said to his companions:
Whoever of our Shi'a and lovers does not have the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays, then he does not have anything of our matter, and he does not know anything of our reasons.

The Imam calls it the **"Alphabet of the Shi'a"** (Abjad al-Shi'a). If the Infallible Imam tells you that this book is your alphabet, do you listen to him, or do you listen to a "Rijal expert" from the 5th or 10th century who wants to "weaken" it because of a rule he borrowed from the enemies of the Ahl al-Bayt?

The Book of Sulaym is the "Mirror of the Tragedy." It documents the **Tears of Ali** and the **Pain of Zahra** in the moments when the "Saqifa" was being built. It is the book that the "New Abbasids" want to burn again, but this time with the "fire of doubt" instead of the wood of Umar. We will not let them. We will use the Book of Sulaym as our primary witness in this file. It is the "Trustworthy and Secure" source that was handed down through the generations. In the next segments, we will read the specific pages where Sulaym describes the **Breaking of the Rib** and the **Martyrdom of Mohsin**.
Segment 5: The Siege of the House

Sheikh al-Ghizzi reads harrowing details from the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays, describing the moment the "party of the Caliphate" surrounded Ali and Fatima's house. He highlights the contrast between the sanctity of the house—saluted by the Prophet every morning—and the brutality of the men who came to violate it. He details the exchange where Lady Fatima tried to shame the attackers and their cold-blooded refusal.

Now, let us open the **Book of Sulaym**. Let us read from the ink that was written while the embers of the fire were still warm in Medina. Sulaym records that after the "Saqifa" was concluded, the order was given to bring Ali ibn Abi Talib by force. The house of Fatima was the target. This house... the house regarding which the Quran says:
In houses which Allah has permitted to be raised and that His name be mentioned therein. [Quran 24:36]
This was the house where the Prophet used to stand at the door for six months, saying: "Peace be upon you, O People of the House!"

Sulaym describes the scene: **Umar ibn al-Khattab** arrived at the head of a large group, carrying fire. He shouted for the house to be opened. Fatima (peace be upon her) came and stood behind the door—she did not open it, but she spoke from behind it. She said to them:
O Umar! What do we have to do with you? Why do you not leave us and our mourning for the Messenger of Allah?
Umar replied:
Open the door, or by Allah, I will burn it over you!
Fatima replied with a heart-breaking question:
O Umar, do you not fear Allah? You enter my house and you violate my home?

But the **Reality of the World** had blinded them. They did not see the "Leader of the Women of Paradise"; they only saw an obstacle to their political project. Sulaym records that Umar called for wood to be piled up against the door.

Imagine the scene: The daughter of the Prophet is inside with her small children—al-Hassan, al-Hussain, and Zaynab—and the "men of the Sahaba" are outside with fire and wood. This is the **Saqifa Justice** that they want you to believe in. Sulaym tells us that Fatima was trying to protect the door, hoping her voice would make them ashamed. She thought the memory of her father was still alive. She did not realize the "Treachery" Ali spoke of had already taken control. This is the beginning of the "First Assault." In the next segment, we will read the most painful part: the **Kicking of the Door** and the **Striking of the Rib**. The File of Fatima is moving from the "Game of Sources" to the **Document of Blood**.
Segment 6: The Assault: The Door, the Rib, and the Whip

Sheikh al-Ghizzi reads from the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays regarding the physical breach of the house. He details the moment Umar kicked the door while Lady Fatima was behind it, crushing her and breaking her rib. He explains the role of Qunfudh, who struck Lady Fatima with a whip, leaving a permanent mark. He emphasizes these "forgotten papers" are avoided by the religious establishment to maintain a sanitized history.

We continue with the text of **Sulaym ibn Qays**. This is the moment where the "Reality of the World" translated its threats into a brutal, physical crime. Sulaym records that Umar, seeing that Fatima would not open the door, called for the fire to be lit. As the wood began to burn, the door was weakened. Sulaym says:

Umar kicked the door with his foot, and it was made of palm branches. Fatima had pressed her body against the door to keep it shut... The door was kicked open, and she was crushed between the door and the wall!
Listen to the description of the eye-witness:

He [Umar] struck her with his sword while it was still in its sheath, and he struck her arm with a whip... She cried out: 'O Father! O Messenger of Allah! How badly have Umar and Abu Bakr treated your family after you!'

This is the **First Wound**. It was not just a political dispute; it was a physical violation of the "Leader of the Women of Paradise." Sulaym records that the blow from the whip was so severe that it left a mark on her arm like a **"bracelet"** (dumluq), which remained until she was placed in her grave.

Then came the role of **Qunfudh**. Sulaym tells us that Umar ordered Qunfudh—a man known for his cruelty—to strike her. Qunfudh struck her between her shoulders, causing her to collapse. It was at this moment that the miscarriage of **Mohsin** occurred. Why do I read these details? Because the **"Worshippers of the Bride"** and the Tusi scholars want to turn this into a "philosophical tragedy." They want to talk about "usurped rights" in a general sense. No! This was **Assault and Battery**. This was the breaking of bones and the spilling of blood.

Sulaym records that Ali ibn Abi Talib, upon hearing her cry, rushed to the door. He seized Umar by his collar and threw him to the ground. He was about to kill him, but he remembered the **Covenant** (Ahd) he had made with the Prophet—the covenant of patience to preserve the outward form of the religion. This is the most difficult paper in the file. It shows the **Patience of Ali** in the face of the **Agony of Fatima**. The "Reality of Religion" required Ali to stay his hand, while the "Reality of the World" was trampling over the sanctity of his home.

The File of Fatima is written in these screams and these blows. If you do not feel this pain, you do not know Zahra. And if you do not know Zahra, you cannot claim to be a "Waitor" for her son. The son of Zahra is coming to ask: "By what sin was she killed?" We must be ready with the answer from the Book of Sulaym.
Segment 7: The Ignorance of the Caliphs

Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents evidence from Sunni sources showing Abu Bakr and Umar were ignorant of basic Quranic vocabulary (e.g., the word "Abb"). He argues that if they didn't know the "External Reality" of the Quran's words, they couldn't know the "Internal Reality" of Lady Fatima’s status, making their usurpation an intellectual and spiritual crime.

We have seen the papers of the "Physical Assault" in the Book of Sulaym. Now, let us look at the **Papers of Intellectual Deficiency**. To understand why they were capable of striking the daughter of the Prophet, you must understand their relationship with the **Knowledge of the Prophet**. We go to the Sunni sources—the books of **Tafsir** and **Hadith**. There is a famous narration recorded by **al-Suyuti**, **al-Tabari**, and others regarding the verse:
And fruits and grass (). [Quran 80:31]
The reports state that **Abu Bakr** was asked about the meaning of the word **"Abb"** in this verse. He replied:
Which sky would shade me and which earth would carry me if I said regarding the Book of Allah that which I do not know?

On the surface, this looks like "piety." But in the **Reality of Religion**, it is a confession of **Ignorance**. If the "Successor of the Prophet" does not know the meaning of a common word for "grass" used in the Quran, how can he be the guardian of the Divine Law?
The same is reported about **Umar ibn al-Khattab**. He was reading the same Surah and said:
We know what the fruit is, but what is the 'Abb'?
Then he struck his staff on the ground and said:
This, by Allah, is the 'Deep Involvement'. What does it matter to you, O Umar, if you do not know what the 'Abb' is?

Look at the **Tusi Mentality**! They turn this into a virtue of "not overthinking." But I ask: Why did they not ask the Gate of Knowledge? They preferred to remain ignorant of the Quran rather than admit their need for the Ahl al-Bayt.

The **"Game of Sources"** works here too. **Bukhari** distorts this Hadith to hide Umar’s ignorance. In some versions, they omit the question entirely to protect the image of the "Learned Caliph." Why is this relevant to the **File of Fatima**? Because one who is ignorant of the **Words of Allah** will inevitably be ignorant of the **Sanctity of the Wali of Allah**. Their ignorance of the word "Abb" is a symbol of their total disconnection from the "Light of the Revelation."

If they did not know the meaning of "grass," how could they know the meaning of **"Purity"** as it applies to Fatima? How could they know the meaning of **"Mawaddah"**? This intellectual vacuum allowed them to commit the crime at the door. They were "blind leaders" who usurped a position requiring "Divine Sight." The File of Fatima documents the **murder of Knowledge** in the Ummah. In the next segment, we will see how this ignorance extended to the basic rules of **Tayammum**.
Segment 8: The Crisis of Jurisprudence

Sheikh al-Ghizzi analyzes the jurisprudential deficiency of the Caliphs using Sahih Muslim. He focuses on an incident where a man asked Umar how to pray without water, and Umar replied: "Do not pray." He contrasts this with the Quranic command regarding Tayammum, arguing this systemic ignorance proves the leadership was unqualified to represent the Prophet.

We move from their ignorance of Quranic vocabulary to their ignorance of the **Pillars of the Prayer**. We go to **Sahih Muslim**, the "Book of Menstruation". There is a narration where a man came to Umar ibn al-Khattab and said:
I have become in a state of major ritual impurity and I cannot find water. What should I do?
Umar replied to him:
Do not pray. (La tusalli!)

Listen to this "fatwa"! The Quran has a clear verse in **Surah al-Ma'idah** and **Surah al-Nisa** commanding the believers that if they cannot find water, they should perform **Tayammum** with clean earth. Ammar ibn Yasir, who was present, was shocked. He reminded Umar of an incident where the Prophet taught them both. But Umar, in his arrogance, told Ammar: "We will let you take responsibility for what you have claimed."

Think about this deeply. If a person is ignorant of **Tayammum**—a basic rule—how can he be the "Inheritor of the Prophet"? If he tells a Muslim to **abandon the prayer**, he is effectively telling him to abandon the connection to Allah. This is the **Internal Darkness** of the Saqifa. When you have a leader disconnected from the "Knowledge of the Revelation," he will naturally be disconnected from the **"Spirit of the Revelation"**—Fatima.

They try to "Tusi-fy" this incident, saying Umar was using "independent reasoning." I say: Can there be **Ijtihad** in the face of a direct Quranic text? This is **Spiritual Bankruptcy**. The **File of Fatima** documents this bankruptcy. One who does not value the prayer will not value the house of the Prophet. One who tells a man to "not pray" will not hesitate to tell his men to "light the fire" at the door of the Zahra. The link between **Ignorance** and **Oppression** is absolute. In the next segment, we will see how this ignorance led to the corruption of the **Form of the Prayer**.
Segment 9: The Lost Prayer

Sheikh al-Ghizzi narrates the story of Sultan Mahmoud al-Ghaznawi and his search for the "Correct Prayer." The story illustrates how centuries of rule by the "Caliphs of the World" distorted the basic ritual of prayer into multiple versions. He argues that if the Ummah lost the form of the prayer, they certainly lost the true station of Lady Fatima.

We move now to a paper from history which reveals the "Intellectual Fruit" of the Saqifa. This is the story of **Sultan Mahmoud al-Ghaznawi**. Sultan Mahmoud found himself in a world of conflicting Sunni schools. He asked: "Which of these is the prayer of the Messenger of Allah?" He gathered the scholars and asked them to perform the prayer. The story records that when the **Hanafi scholar** performed the prayer according to strict rules—including "minimal requirements"—the Sultan was horrified.

The Sultan saw that the prayer had become a "skeleton" without a spirit. He realized the **"Reality of the World"** had invaded the most sacred ritual. Why do I bring this story in the **File of Fatima**? Because the Tusi establishment wants you to believe the "Sunni/Shi'a dispute" is just a minor difference in law. No! It is a dispute over the **Reality of the Revelation**.

If they could not preserve the **Prayer**, how could they preserve the **Truth of Fatima**, which was hidden behind the door? The distortion of the prayer is the proof of the distortion of the **Leadership**. Imam Ali said: "I am the one who established the prayer." When he took the Caliphate, people said: "Ali has reminded us of the prayer of the Messenger, which we had not seen since his death!" This means for a quarter of a century, the Ummah was praying a "distorted prayer." They were living in a spiritual void. It was in this **Darkness of Knowledge** that the crime against Zahra was committed.
Segment 10: The Divine Witness

Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents a "Paper of Divine Judgment." He recounts the incident where Imam Ali called upon companions to testify regarding the Hadith of Ghadir Khumm. Anas ibn Malik claimed he had "forgotten." Ali prayed for a divine sign to expose the lie, resulting in Anas being afflicted with leprosy. This served as a permanent testimony to the betrayal of the "Truth" and the "File of Fatima."

We move now to the **"Lost Testimony."** To understand the File of Fatima, you must understand the **Culture of Betrayal** among those who were supposed to be the witnesses of the Truth. After the death of the Prophet, Imam Ali stood in the mosque and said:
I adjure by Allah every person who heard the Messenger say at Ghadir Khumm: 'Of whomsoever I am master, Ali is his master,' to stand up and testify.
Many stood up. But **Anas ibn Malik**, the servant of the Prophet, remained seated. Ali said:
O Anas, what prevents you from testifying? You were there.
Anas replied with the excuse of the "Reality of the World":
O Commander of the Faithful, I have grown old, and I have forgotten.
He had not forgotten; he was afraid of the ruling authorities. Ali prayed:
O Allah! If he is lying, strike him with a white mark [leprosy] that no turban can hide!

The narrators record that before Anas could leave, a white spot appeared on his face. He lived the rest of his life as a **Leper**. Why is this paper in the **File of Fatima**? Because the same "forgetfulness" of Anas is what allowed the Ummah to "forget" the broken rib. If they could lie about the public declaration of Ghadir, they would lie about what happened in the privacy of a home. The leprosy of Anas is the **Physical Document of Betrayal**. It is a sign from Allah that the "Reality of Religion" will manifest on the bodies of those who hide it.
Segment 11: The Jurisprudence of Opposition

Sheikh al-Ghizzi discusses the total divergence between the path of the Ahl al-Bayt and the established political-religious "Reality of the World." He explains why the Imams taught "The Truth is found in opposing the opposers." He turns to the Prophet's final moments, emphasizing his absolute decree: "I am not pleased until Fatima is pleased." He ends with the Prophet weeping for the future striking of Fatima’s cheek.

We reach the final papers of Episode 2. We discussed the "Physical Assault" and the "Intellectual Deficiency" of those who seized authority. Now, we must understand the **Resulting Jurisprudential Rift**. The Tusi scholars want to say the differences are minor. But our Imams gave us a rule for when we are confused by conflicting reports:
See which of the two opinions matches the 'Amma', and take the one that opposes them, for the Truth lies in opposing them.

Why? Because the official "Reality of the World" was built upon **Distorting the Truth** to justify the Saqifa. If the Caliphs changed the prayer and fasting to suit their politics, then the "Reality of Religion" must be found in the opposite. Take the **Prayer of Imam Ali**. People said: "By Allah, Ali has prayed with us the prayer of the Messenger, which we had not seen since his death!" For twenty-five years, the Ummah had forgotten how to pray correctly!

If they distorted the **Prayer**, what did they do to the **Status of Fatima**? They reduced her to a "disputant over inheritance" instead of the "Leader of the Women of Paradise." The "Truth" went with the Ahl al-Bayt into the corners of their homes. This is why our jurisprudence is a **Jurisprudence of Opposition**. We oppose to find the original, unpolluted spring that the "Sultans' scholars" tried to poison.
Finally, we go to the **Final Satisfaction of the Prophet**. The Prophet said to Fatima:
By Allah, O Fatima! I am not pleased until you are pleased. Then again, I am not pleased until you are pleased!

This is a **Divine Decree**. Did Fatima die pleased with Abu Bakr and Umar? **Bukhari** says she died angry with them. If she was angry, the Prophet is not pleased. If the Prophet is not pleased, Allah is not pleased.
The Prophet wept in his final hours and said:
I weep for what will be done to you after me. I weep for the striking of Fatima on her cheek... for the violating of her sanctity.

He said: "The limit has been crossed by the striking of the cheek." This is the "File of Fatima" in its rawest form. The Prophet saw the hand of the oppressor rising against his daughter before it ever happened.

title: The File of Fatima - Episode 3: The Crime Documents
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 3

In this episode, titled **"The Crime Documents,"** Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents a devastating collection of primary textual evidence documenting the systematic assault on Lady Fatima (SA). He moves beyond general history into specific "Crime Papers," utilizing the **Ziyarah Jami'a for the Imams of the Believers** as a historical witness to the betrayal. The episode covers the "Mockery of Forgiveness" (the visit of the Caliphs to her deathbed), the role of **Qunfudh** as a rewarded perpetrator, and the "Tragedy of the Grave," where the authorities attempted to exhume her body. The Sheikh asserts that these documents prove Lady Fatima was not just a victim of a political dispute, but a martyr of a physical and premeditated assault.
Segment 1: The Testimony of the Ziyarah

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens by reciting from the Ziyarah Jami'a for the Imams of the Believers. He explains that this text is not just a prayer but a historical documentation of the crimes committed after the Prophet's death. The Ziyarah explicitly mentions the snatching of the Caliphate, the violation of the "House of Prophecy," and the wounding of the Prophet’s heart through the persecution of his daughter.

Peace be upon Fatima the chosen one and al-Murtada, her husband and the Infallibles from her children. **The File of Fatima - Episode 3.** I open my words with what came in the **Ziyarah Jami'a for the Imams of the Believers**. This is different from the "Great Ziyarah Jami'a" and is found in **Mafatih al-Jinan** a few pages after it. In this Ziyarah, we address the Household of Infallibility:
O my masters, O Family of the Messenger of Allah, I seek nearness to Allah... by opposing those who betrayed you, broke their oath of allegiance, and denied your Wilayah.
...those who abandoned the paths of your love... and sought closeness to their Pharaonic rulers by disassociating from you.
The text continues:
When the Chosen One passed away... they snatched the opportunity, violated the sanctity, and abandoned him on his deathbed while they hurried to break the oath and betray the trust.
...they headed to the 'House of Prophecy,' the landing place of the Revelation and the Angels... until they broke the Prophet's covenant regarding his brother Ali.

...they wounded the liver of the Best of Creation by the oppression of his daughter, the persecution of his beloved, and the humiliation of his dear one... they humiliated her after him and belittled her status.
Segment 2: The Martyrdom of the Progeny

The Sheikh continues the recitation, describing the tragic fate of the Imams as a direct consequence of the first betrayal. He transitions to "The Great Crime"—the martyrdom of Lady Fatima. He mentions historical accounts of those who stood outside her house weeping when they heard her cries, illustrating that while many felt the horror of the event, the political reality suppressed any intervention.
O my masters... the arrows of the Ummah were soaked in your livers, their spears were pointed at your necks, and their swords were drinking your blood.
...you were either struck in the Mihrab with a sword to the head, or a martyr whose shroud was pierced by arrows, or killed in Iraq [Hussain] with his head raised on a spear.
...or imprisoned with limbs crushed by iron, or poisoned with intestines severed by a draft of venom.

This is Fatima and her children and those are her enemies. Their names are clear and their persons are known. Today’s talk is about another aspect of the File: the "Great Crime"—the murder of the daughter of the Prophet. I cannot gather all documents in one episode, but I will present the "Crime Documents" that define the tragedy. We see in the documents that when Fatima cried out behind the door, there were people outside who began to weep. They heard her voice being raised in pain. Yet the political "Reality of the World" was so brutal that even their tears did not move the perpetrators to stop.
Segment 3: The Mockery of Seeking Forgiveness

Sheikh al-Ghizzi details the visit of Abu Bakr and Umar to Lady Fatima’s deathbed. He describes her absolute rejection of them, noting that she only allowed them entry because Imam Ali asked her. He explains that she refused to look at them or reply to their greetings, using this as a "Document of Eternal Displeasure" that invokes the Prophet’s warning: "Whoever angers Fatima, angers me."

The documents record that Abu Bakr and Umar requested Ali to ask permission for them to visit Fatima. She refused them repeatedly. Eventually, she allowed them in only because Ali asked her. When they entered, they offered their salutations, but **she turned her face away toward the wall**. She did not reply to their greeting.

In the **"Reality of Religion,"** if a person does not reply to a Salam, it is a sign of total rejection. She was declaring that they were no longer part of her spiritual world. They spoke of their "closeness" to the Prophet, but she reminded them of what the Prophet had said:
Fatima is a piece of me; whoever angers her, angers me.
Segment 4: The Final Testimony and the Miscarriage

The Sheikh translates Lady Fatima’s final address to the two visitors, where she vows to complain against them to the Messenger of Allah. He presents evidence from both Shi'a and Sunni sources for the miscarriage of Mohsen (AS), specifically citing Al-Shahrastani to show that the "physical pressure" of the door was the recognized cause of the child's death.
She said to them:
I adjure you by Allah, did you not hear the Messenger say that my anger is his anger?
They admitted they had. She then raised her hands and said:
O Allah, I call You to witness that these two have angered me and have not pleased me. I shall complain against them to my father when I meet him.

Now we look at the document of **Mohsen**. The miscarriage of Mohsen is mentioned in both Shi'a and Sunni sources (such as **Al-Milal wa al-Nihal**). They try to hide this by saying "it was a natural miscarriage," but the "Crime Documents" show it was a result of the **Crushing** behind the door.
Segment 5: The Letter of Umar and the Firewood

Sheikh al-Ghizzi introduces the "Letter of Umar to Mu'awiyah" as a primary confession. In this letter, Umar reportedly admits to the physical brutality used at the door. The Sheikh also highlights the terrifying detail that the firewood was not merely for a "threat," but was gathered in a volume sufficient to burn the entire house and everyone within it.

You must know the **Letter of Umar to Mu'awiyah**. It is a confession. He describes how he pressed the door against her until her ribs snapped. Another document states that the amount of firewood collected was enough to burn the **entire house**, not just the door. This shows a "Genocidal Intent"—they were willing to burn the Household of the Prophet to secure their pledge of allegiance.
Segment 6: Spitting on the Prophet’s Words

The Sheikh discusses the confrontation between Imam Hassan (AS) and Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba, where the Imam explicitly accused Mughira of striking Lady Fatima. He then links this to the "Tragedy of Thursday," where the Prophet's request for a pen and paper was met with the claim that he was "delirious," which the Sheikh frames as the first step in the systematic rejection of the Prophet's daughter.
Imam Hassan stood before Mu'awiyah and his court. He looked at **Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba** and said:
You are the one who struck Fatima until she miscarried... so that she died as a result of that blow.
This is a public accusation by an Infallible Imam. The document of the "Tragedy of Thursday" shows that when the Prophet asked for a pen and paper to write a decree, Umar said:
The man is delirious.
This was the first "strike" against Lady Fatima—the rejection of her father's authority.
Segment 7: Fatima’s Own Testimony

Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents Lady Fatima’s own account of her oppression as recorded in texts like Dala'il al-Imama. He reads her description of the burning door and the violation of her home, arguing that her testimony is the most definitive "Crime Paper" in the entire file.
Fatima (SA) said:
They gathered wood at my door and brought fire to burn me and my children... while I was standing behind the door.
She describes how the whip of Qunfudh left a mark on her arm that remained until her death. This is her own testimony. It is recorded in **Dala'il al-Imama** and other ancient texts.
Segment 8: The Attempt to Exhume the Grave

The Sheikh details the "Tragedy After Death." He recounts how the authorities, furious about her secret night-burial, planned to dig up the graves in Baqi' to perform a public funeral. He describes Imam Ali’s armed intervention, which forced them to retreat, thus preserving the secret location of her grave as a permanent symbol of her protest.
When they woke up and found she was buried, Abu Bakr said:
We must dig up the graves of Medina until we find her!
Ali (AS) came out in his yellow turban of war, holding **Zulfiqar**, and said:
By Allah, if a single stone of these graves is moved, I will strike the necks of those who did it!
They knew Ali did not make idle threats. They backed down, and her grave remains hidden as a permanent document of her anger against them.
Segment 9: The Alphabet of the Crime: Sulaym ibn Qays

Returning to the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays, the Sheikh calls it the "Alphabet of the Crime." He provides a chronological reading of the physical breach, emphasizing that this eye-witness account provides the specific mechanics of the assault that the later scholastic establishment tried to "weaken."
We return to the **Book of Sulaym**. It is the most important document because it provides the chronology. Sulaym says:
Umar called for the fire, then he kicked the door... I saw the door break and Fatima cry out... Ali was seized and led away with a rope around his neck.
This is the "Reality of Religion" documented by the first of the Shi'a historians.
Segment 10: The Whip of Qunfudh

Sheikh al-Ghizzi details the role of Qunfudh and a specific historical anomaly: why he was the only governor not fined by the Caliph. He cites Imam Ali’s explanation that the exempting of Qunfudh's wealth was a "reward" for the specific blow he delivered to Lady Fatima that eventually led to her death.
Imam Ali was asked:
Why did Umar fine all his governors but leave Qunfudh?
Ali replied:
He did that to thank him for the blow he struck Fatima with his whip... which broke her arm and led to her death.
This is the "Crime Document" that shows the conspiracy. The attackers were rewarded for their brutality.
Segment 11: Conclusion and the Eternal File

The Sheikh concludes by asserting that the evidence presented confirms a "premeditated conspiracy" to murder the Prophet's daughter. He tells the viewers that the "File of Fatima" will remain open until the Mahdi appears to bring final justice, and asks them to reflect on which "Reality" they choose to belong to.
The text of Sulaym says:
Umar pushed the door and Fatima stood before him... he raised his sword in its sheath and struck her side... she cried 'O Father!'... then he raised the whip and struck her arm.

This is the image that every Shi'a must carry. The File of Fatima is the file of her oppression. It is a continuous reality. We will continue to reveal more documents in the coming episodes. I ask you for prayers. In the protection of Allah.

title: The File of Fatima - Episode 4: The Criminals
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 4

In this episode, titled **"The Criminals,"** Sheikh al-Ghizzi investigates the specific individuals and tactics used to enforce the post-prophetic leadership. He details the **"State of Terror"** in Medina, where the **Aslam Tribe** (Bedouins) was utilized as a militia to force the population into submission. The Sheikh provides a graphic reconstruction of Imam Ali (AS) being dragged to the mosque and explores the intellectual resistance of the **12 Companions** who publicly challenged the first Caliph. The episode concludes with a harrowing examination of the perpetrators of the physical assault, specifically **Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba**, and a reading of Lady Fatima’s (SA) own description of her injuries.
Segment 1: The Haul of Imam Ali

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens by describing the immediate aftermath of the attack on the house. He reads from the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays, detailing how Imam Ali (AS) was "hauled" (dragged with extreme violence) to the mosque. He lists the primary figures surrounding the first Caliph, all of whom were heavily armed, creating an atmosphere of military intimidation rather than religious consensus.

**The File of Fatima - Episode 4.** I am still browsing and turning the papers of this file. In this episode, my talk will be in three areas. The first area: we will wander through the texts to see the atmosphere in which the oppression of Fatima (peace be upon her) occurred. We will take a general look at what came in the texts that are in our hands. The texts are many; I will pick and focus on the most important of these texts which, in their totality, draw for us a clear picture of the general atmosphere that was surrounding that crime which occurred.

The first text I deal with is from the **Book of Sulaym ibn Qays**. I read this text to you in the previous episode. In this episode, I take from it the point of need that relates to giving us a glimpse or a picture of the atmosphere surrounding the reality of the crime. Page 587 of the second volume of the Book of Sulaym ibn Qays (verified by Sheikh Muhammad Baqir al-Ansari).
On page 587 of this important document:

Then they set off with Ali (peace be upon him) when they forced him out of his house. Then they set off with Ali (peace be upon him) being hauled... until they reached Abu Bakr, with Umar standing with a sword over his [Abu Bakr's] head.
"Then they set off with Ali **being hauled**"—meaning dragged with power, with violence, and with intensity by a large group of people. The picture becomes clear to us little by little.

Umar was standing with the sword over the head of Abu Bakr, along with **Khalid ibn al-Walid, Abu Ubaidah ibn al-Jarrah, Salim (the slave of Abu Hudhayfa), Mu'adh ibn Jabal, al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba, Usaid ibn Hudayr, and Bashir ibn Sa'd**. All of these were surrounding Abu Bakr, and the rest of the people were sitting around Abu Bakr **carrying weapons**. This was a military operation to secure a pledge by force. There were weapons. There was a group that brought him from the house, and there was a group with Abu Bakr, and all of them were armed. There were large numbers of people.
Segment 2: The Terrorism of the Streets

Using Bihar al-Anwar as a source, the Sheikh describes the "Raid" (al-Kabs) tactics used by the new authorities. He details how Umar and his group patrolled Medina, breaking into the homes of those who refused to pledge allegiance and dragging them to the mosque. He argues that this systemic state terrorism was a calculated effort to paralyze any opposition to the Saqifa.
This is **Bihar al-Anwar**, Volume 28. On page 204, Hadith number 3. I take these lines from this report from Abdullah ibn Abdul-Rahman:

Then Umar tightened his belt and began roaming Medina. He was calling out: 'Abu Bakr has been pledged allegiance to, so come to the pledge!' So people rushed to pledge. Umar knew that a group was hiding in their houses. He used to head to them in a group (jam') and raid them.

He knew there were those from the Ansar and Muhajireen who did not want to pledge and had hidden in their homes. He used to head to them in a group and **raid them**—meaning he entered their houses by force.

This **"Raid"** (al-Kabs)—it wasn't just the house of al-Zahra that was raided, but the biggest issue was in the house of al-Zahra. He raided them, meaning he entered upon them in their homes by force and arrested them. You notice that Medina was in a state of extreme tension. He would raid them and bring them to the mosque to pledge. This is a **pledge under coercion**. Why else were they in their houses and brought to the mosque by force?
Until when days had passed, he approached in a great multitude.

Notice the description. When he went to the houses of the Ansar and Muhajireen, he went in a "group." But when he went to the house of al-Zahra, he went in a **"great multitude"** to the house of Ali ibn Abi Talib and demanded he come out, and he refused.
Umar came with fire and said:
By Him in whose hand is the soul of Umar, you shall come out or I will burn it [the house] down on whoever is in it!
He would burn the house, not just the door, and everyone in it: Fatima, her husband, and her sons.
Segment 3: The Bedouin Siege

The Sheikh explains the strategic role of the Aslam Tribe in securing the Caliphate. He describes how thousands of Bedouins were promised supplies in exchange for acting as an enforcement militia. Their presence in the streets of Medina turned the city into a zone of fear, effectively neutralizing the Prophet's companions who wished to support Imam Ali.
Umar utilized the **Aslam Tribe** that had entered Medina to get supplies. He said to them:
If you help us secure the pledge for the successor of the Prophet, we will give you what you need.

These Bedouins filled the streets and alleys of Medina. Anyone who saw them was terrified. They would strike anyone who refused to pledge. This was the "Reality of the World." The city of the Prophet was under a military siege by desert tribes who had no understanding of the sanctity of the Prophet's family.
Segment 4: The Debate of the 12 Companions

Sheikh al-Ghizzi details the "Intellectual Resistance" led by 12 prominent companions, including Salman, Abu Dharr, and Ammar. He describes how they stood in the mosque and used the Prophet's own words to challenge Abu Bakr's legitimacy. Despite their courage, the authorities used the threat of the sword to silence them, marking the definitive end of public dissent in Medina.

Twelve men from the Muhajireen and Ansar stood up to debate Abu Bakr. Among them were **Salman, Abu Dharr, al-Miqdad, and Ammar**. They reminded the people of Ghadir Khumm and the Prophet's decree. One by one, they stood and gave their testimony. But the authorities responded with threats of the sword. This debate is a **"Document of Truth"** that shows that the elite companions of the Prophet never accepted the legitimacy of the Saqifa.
Segment 5: The Crimes of Mughira ibn Shu'ba

The Sheikh focuses on the character and actions of Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba, one of the most violent figures in the assault. He cites documents stating that Mughira's blow made Lady Fatima bleed. He also touches upon Mughira's controversial background and a reading of Lady Fatima’s (SA) own description of her injuries to show the low character of the men used to enforce the new rule.

There is a specific document stating that **Mughira ibn Shu'ba struck Fatima until she bled**. This is the same Mughira who was accused of **Zina** (adultery) and was only saved from the legal punishment by a technicality provided by Umar. These were the "men of the state"—criminals and opportunists who were used to strike the daughter of the Messenger of Allah.
From another page (582):
And the nail of the door was driven into her chest. Fatima cried out: 'O Father! O Messenger of Allah!'... I do not know the news of the nail—ask her chest, the treasury of secrets.
She cried: 'O Father! See what we have met after you from the son of Khattab and the son of Abu Quhafa!'
Umar turned to those around him and said: 'Strike Fatima!' Whips rained down on the beloved of the Messenger of Allah and his piece.
This is not my talk; this is from **Awalim al-Ulum** (page 582).
As I read in the previous episode from her testament:

They gathered the dense firewood at our door and brought fire to burn it and burn the People of the House. I stood by the side of the door and adjured them by Allah... Umar took the whip from the hand of Qunfudh and struck my arm with it. The whip wrapped around my arm until it became like a bracelet.

He kicked the door with his foot and pushed it against me while I was pregnant. I fell on my face while the fire was blazing... He struck me with his hand until my earring broke. The labor pains came to me and I miscarried Mohsen, killed without a crime.

I bear witness that whoever connected with you connected with the Messenger, and whoever cut you off, O Mother of al-Hassan, cut off the Messenger. O Fatima, the pure, the purified the Zahr'a of Muhammad you are the lamp of the Throne. I hope that your love remains with me in my life, in my grave, and in my hereafter. Peace be upon you, O Fatimids. I ask you all for prayers. In the protection of Allah.

title: The File of Fatima - Episode 5: Slanders and Lies
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 5

In this fifth episode, titled **"Slanders and Lies,"** Sheikh al-Ghizzi examines the foundational fabrications and "theological lies" used to justify the oppression of Lady Fatima (SA). He argues that the betrayal of the Prophet's daughter began with a series of false narratives—such as the claim that "Prophets do not leave inheritance" or that "The Ummah cannot unite on error." The episode delves into the specific history of **Fadak**, the plot to assassinate Imam Ali (AS) in the mosque, and the systemic abuse of Lady Fatima’s character throughout history by various dynasties, including the Umayyads and Abbasids. The Sheikh frames these lies as a "chain of falsehood" that continues to this day, used by the "Reality of the World" to obscure the "Reality of Religion."
Segment 1: The Chain of Falsehood

Sheikh al-Ghizzi opens with a recitation from the Ziyarah of the الصديقة الكبرى (The Great Truthful One), emphasizing her titles as "The Oppressed," "The One whose rights were usurped," and "The One whose rib was broken." He establishes that the "First Paper" in the File of Fatima is titled "Slanders and Lies." He argues that the deviation of the Ummah began with fabrications against the Prophet, such as the false claim that Prophets do not leave inheritance, and the invention of rules like "The Ummah does not unite on error" to justify the coup at Saqifa.

Peace be upon Fatima al-Mustafa and al-Murtada after her and the Infallibles from her children and her sons. Peace be upon the Fatimids in every time and place, from her descendants, her Shi'a, and her lovers, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings. **The File of Fatima.** Another episode of the segments of the File of Fatima. New papers I am turning, browsing, and researching.

I begin my talk in this episode with a passage from the **Ziyarah of the Great Truthful One** (Siddiqa al-Kubra), peace and blessings be upon her. This is from **Mafatih al-Jinan**. What do the phrases of this Ziyarah say?
O Allah, bless Muhammad and his Household, and bless the Purified Batool, the Infallible Truthful One... the Oppressed, the Overpowered, the One whose rights were usurped.
The One whose rights were usurped, the One prevented from her inheritance, the One whose rib was broken, the One whose husband was oppressed, the One whose son was killed.
