Segment 8: The Palm Tree in the Dunghill - The Insult to the Prophet's Lineage

The Sheikh unravels a highly fragmented and censored story found in Sunni books (Majma' al-Zawa'id, Tirmidhi, Tafsir al-Tabari). These sources cryptically mention an unnamed Quraishi man who insulted the Prophet by saying he was like "a palm tree growing in a dunghill," which deeply angered the Prophet, leading him to defend his pure lineage on the pulpit. The Sunni texts mysteriously show Umar suddenly begging the Prophet for forgiveness without explaining his role. To reveal the full, unedited truth, the Sheikh turns to Kitab Sulaym ibn Qays, which explicitly records that it was Umar who made this insulting remark directly to Imam Ali. This provoked the Prophet's intense anger and a definitive declaration of his and Ali's absolute purity and divine selection, ultimately forcing Umar to desperately plead for pardon.

I do not want to branch out; there are other demands, you notice I pointed to them here, several demands. But I find the time passing quickly, I leave them for another time, for another occasion. Other demands in which the mention of Ali is hidden. Many truths are present in the books of the people in which the mention of Ali is hidden. And what is the guilt of Ali? And what is the crime of Ali? Except that he is Ali, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him! The crime of Ali is that he is Ali, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. Ali in his essence, Ali in his traits, Ali in his actions, Ali in every aspect of his affairs, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. I brought an example. It was my desire to branch out in bringing other examples of hiding the mention of Ali, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, but I leave this matter to another time.

I said before I enter into clarifying this example, or clarifying this matter, what I brought of referents of hiding the mention of Ali, I said there is another issue that comes in the context of the manners of Caliph Umar and his dealing with the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family. There is an issue mentioned in the books of hadith. I will start my speech from here. An issue mentioned in the books of hadith, and this issue came in several sources. From these sources... these are pictures, these are pictures from the book **Majma' al-Zawa'id**. The source from which I transmit the report, the copy was not available with me, so these are pictures. Majma' al-Zawa'id wa Manba' al-Fawa'id by the memorizer al-Haythami. A known book from the known books, Dar al-Fikr for Printing, Publishing, and Distribution. Printed in the year 1994, verified by Abdullah Muhammad al-Darwish. This is the eighth volume, and this is page 366. Quickly, Hadith 13823. There is a report:
A man from the people said: Indeed the likeness of Muhammad among Bani Hashim is like a sweet basil in the middle of filth (Natn).
Filth, meaning impurities, dirts. 13823: "So a man from the people said: Indeed the likeness of Muhammad among Bani Hashim is like a sweet basil in the middle of filth."

On page 397, Hadith 13824: "People from the Ansar came to the Prophet and said: 'We hear from your people'—from your people, meaning from Quraish, these are from the Ansar, so there is someone from the Muhajirun—'We hear from your people, to the extent that a speaker from them says:
The likeness of Muhammad is only like a palm tree that grew in a dunghill (Al-Kiba or Al-Kuba).'

Al-Kiba, meaning the garbage dump. "People from the Ansar came," with his chain of narration, the details are present in the books. "People from the Ansar came and said: 'We hear...'" notice the previous hadith: "So a man from the people said: Indeed the likeness of Muhammad among Bani Hashim is like a sweet basil in the middle of filth." Here a people from the Ansar came and said: "We hear from your people a speaker from Quraish say: The likeness of Muhammad is only like a palm tree that grew in a dunghill." The dunghill, the sweeping place, meaning the garbage dump.

Al-Tirmidhi also narrated this hadith. This is **Sunan al-Tirmidhi**, page 956. This is the Dar Ihya' al-Turath al-Arabi edition, al-Tirmidhi deceased in the year 297 of the Hijrah, and this is the first edition, page 956, Hadith 36116. From al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib, with his chain of narration, he said: "I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, indeed Quraish—Quraish, your people—sat and remembered their lineages among themselves.
So they made your likeness like a palm tree in a rubbish heap (Kabwah) of the earth.'

The rubbish heap (Kabwah), meaning the place where dirt accumulates. The same thing, the sweeping place, the dunghill. "The likeness of Muhammad is only like a palm tree that grew in a dunghill." Ahmad also narrated it, and its men, the men of this hadith, are the men of the Sahih. "Ahmad narrated it, and its men are the men of the Sahih." And al-Tabarani narrated it in Al-Kabir also, volume 20, page 206.

In the same Majma' al-Zawa'id, page 398, they say that the Prophet said this: "My likeness and the likeness of my Ahlulbayt is like the likeness of a palm tree that grew in a garbage dump." This is a hadith they fabricate against the Messenger of Allah! They say that the Prophet said thus: "My likeness and the likeness of my Ahlulbayt is like the likeness of a palm tree that grew in a garbage dump." Al-Tabarani narrated it also, in addition to Majma' al-Zawa'id, and other sources also mentioned this hadith about the Prophet. And from Ibn al-Zubayr: "That Quraish said: Indeed the likeness of Muhammad is like a palm tree in a rubbish heap."

Who is the one who said this saying? There are other sources: Al-Ansab by al-Sam'ani, the first volume, page 25: "The likeness of Muhammad is only like the likeness of a palm tree that grew in a dunghill." Al-Mustadrak ala al-Sahihayn: "The likeness of Muhammad is only like the likeness of a palm tree that grew in a sweeping place." And other sources also exist from their books, Al-Musnad al-Jami' volume 12, page 129. Many numerous other sources mentioned this hadith.

There is a narration present in Majma' al-Zawa'id, I will read the narration to you. You noticed that someone from Quraish spoke with this high etiquette about the Prophet, that his likeness is like the likeness of a palm tree that grew in a garbage dump, in a sweeping place. The speaker is from Quraish. You notice all the hadiths pointed to this. Who is he? There is a narration from Ibn Abbas, Hadith number 1382. "A son of Safiyyah, the aunt of the Messenger of Allah, died. So she cried over him and wailed. So the Prophet came to her and said: 'O aunt, what makes you cry?' She said: 'My son died.' He said: 'O aunt, whoever has a child die in Islam and is patient, Allah builds for him a house in Paradise.' So she went silent. Then she went out from the Messenger of Allah, and Umar ibn al-Khattab met her." This is Majma' al-Zawa'id, volume eight, page 398. "Then she went out from the Messenger of Allah, and Umar ibn al-Khattab met her.
So he said: 'O Safiyyah, I heard your crying! Indeed your relation to the Messenger of Allah... Indeed your relation to the Messenger of Allah will not avail you against Allah anything!'

So she cried. So the Prophet heard her, and he used to honor her and love her. So he said: 'O aunt, are you crying when I have told you what I told you?'" Because he said to her a short while ago: "Whoever has a child die in Islam and is patient, Allah builds for him a house in Paradise," so she went silent. "So he said: 'O aunt, are you crying when I have told you what I told you?' She said: 'That is not what made me cry, O Messenger of Allah. Umar ibn al-Khattab met me and said: Indeed your relation to the Messenger of Allah will not avail you against Allah anything!'
He said: So the Prophet became angry, and said: 'O Bilal, call for prayer!'

"So Bilal called for prayer. So the Prophet ascended the pulpit, praised Allah and extolled Him, then said: 'What is the matter with people who claim that my relation does not benefit? Every tie and lineage is cut off on the Day of Resurrection except my tie and my lineage, for they are connected in this world and the Hereafter.' This hadith is during the days of the Messenger of Allah. So Umar said: 'So I married Umm Kulthum.'" According to what they say, that he married Umm Kulthum the daughter of Ali, may Allah be pleased with them both, "because of what I heard from the Messenger of Allah on that day." You notice the contradiction in the speech! That he married Umm Kulthum because of what he heard on that day. How much is the duration between the days of his caliphate and this hadith? "I loved that I have a tie and lineage from him." A sayer might say that he remained influenced by this hadith until the opportunity arose for him in the days of his caliphate. But let us continue. The hadith continues.
"Then I went out from the Messenger of Allah"—and Umar says:

So I passed by a group from Quraish, and they were boasting and remembering the affair of the pre-Islamic era. So I said: 'Who is the Messenger of Allah?' They said: 'Indeed the tree grows in the dunghill!'

The strange thing is that the hadith in its beginning is from Ibn Abbas, then it shifted to Umar! "Then I went out from the Messenger of Allah, so I passed by a group from Quraish, and they were boasting and remembering the affair of the pre-Islamic era. So I said: 'Who is the Messenger of Allah?' They said: 'Indeed the tree grows in the dunghill!'" He said: "So I passed to the Prophet and informed him! So he said: 'O Bilal, call for prayer!' So he praised Allah and extolled Him. Then he said: 'O people, who am I?' They said: 'You are the Messenger of Allah.' He said: 'State my lineage.' They said: 'You are Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib.' He said: 'Yes, I am Muhammad ibn Abdullah, and I am the Messenger of Allah. So what is the matter with people who disparage my origin? By Allah, I am indeed the best of them in origin, and the finest of them in position.' So when the Ansar heard that, they said: 'Rise and take weapons, for the Messenger of Allah has been angered.' He said: So they took weapons, then came to the Prophet, nothing was seen of them except the pupils of their eyes." Except the pupils, because they were veiled or covered in iron. "Until they surrounded the people, and made them like the Harrah (volcanic field), until the doors of the mosques and the streets became narrow with them. Then they stood between the hands of the Messenger of Allah and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, do not command us regarding anyone except that we sever his lineage!' So when the group from Quraish saw that, they stood up to the Messenger of Allah and apologized and sought to excuse themselves. So the Messenger of Allah said: 'The people are an outer garment, and the Ansar are an inner garment.' So he praised them and said good."

The confusion is clear in the whole story! The story in the beginning, Ibn Abbas talks about Safiyyah the aunt of the Messenger of Allah, her son died and she came crying. So the Messenger of Allah said to her that whoever is patient, Allah builds for him a house in Paradise. She went out, Umar met her, so he said to her: "Your relation to the Messenger of Allah will not avail you against Allah anything." So she cried. She informed the Messenger. So the Prophet stood preaching. He said, "Call for prayer, O Bilal." Directly Umar spoke here, he said that I married Umm Kulthum the daughter of Ali for this reason! This hadith is during the days of the Prophet, the marriage is during the days of the caliphate, if the marriage is true. Then he says: "I passed by a people from Quraish and they said that the Messenger of Allah is like the tree that grew in the dunghill." And he returned and informed the Prophet! So a second time the Prophet went out and called for prayer! The issue... the fabrication is obvious in it, and the confusion is in it. And this is a very important incident if we want to trace, to notice how the Companions used to speak with the Prophet, how they describe the Prophet that he is like a tree in a garbage dump!

Now, if we return... if we return to the books of hadith and the books of exegesis, we trace what came in them. Even in Sahih al-Bukhari! Even in Sahih al-Bukhari, we can find something related to this subject. On page 814 according to the edition present with me, Hadith 4621-4622, Book of the Exegesis of the Quran, Chapter of His saying: "Do not ask about things which, if they are shown to you, will distress you." Of course, here the reports are mutilated and summarized. From Anas, may Allah be pleased with him, Hadith 4621: He said:
The Messenger of Allah delivered a sermon the likes of which I have never heard, ever!

Why did he say he delivered a sermon the likes of which he had never heard? Meaning, was this sermon from the aspect of words? Never! The Messenger of Allah, knowledge is knowledge, and eloquence is eloquence, and the beauty of speech is the beauty of speech in any place and any time. But the circumstances surrounding the sermon are what made him say this speech. "The Messenger of Allah delivered a sermon the likes of which I have never heard, ever!" And he says: "He said: 'If you knew what I know, you would laugh little and weep much.' He said:
So the companions of the Messenger of Allah covered their faces, they had a sobbing sound (Khanin)!
And in other copies, they had a yearning sound (Hanin). Why are they covering their faces?

Let us read the speech. Anas says: "The Messenger of Allah delivered a sermon the likes of which I have never heard, ever!" This is Sahih al-Bukhari, and upon his method, how he cuts the hadiths, the picture will become clear to us bit by bit. "The Messenger of Allah delivered a sermon the likes of which I have never heard, ever! He said: 'If you knew what I know, you would laugh little and weep much.' He said: So the companions of the Messenger of Allah covered their faces, they had a sobbing sound or a yearning sound.
So a man said: 'Who is my father?'

What is the point of evidence here? Or what is the reason a man stands up and says "Who is my father?" There are missing gaps! "So a man said: 'Who is my father?' He said: 'So-and-so.' So this verse descended: 'Do not ask about things which, if they are shown to you, will distress you.'"

And of course, there are reports on this pattern of fabrication and cutting. But what do we read in this hadith, and the hadith after it is like it too? The Prophet delivered a sermon, as Anas says, "Anas never heard the likes of it." The Companions covered their heads, their faces. A man stood up and said, "Who is my father?" Is there anything clear in this speech? With al-Bukhari, upon the method of deception, cutting, and hiding the truths, there is nothing clear. But if we go searching in the books of the people, the truth will become clear to us bit by bit, and yet a large part of the truth will remain buried.

This is **Tafsir al-Tabari**. And this is the seventh/eighth volume, Dar Ihya' al-Turath al-Arabi, commentary by Mahmoud Shakir, page 98. In the tail of the verse: "O you who have believed, do not ask about things which, if they are shown to you, will distress you," verse 101 from Surah al-Ma'idah. In the tail of the verse, the hadith is from Abu Huraira:

His face is reddened from the intensity of anger. "Until he sat on the pulpit. So a man stood to him and said: 'Where is my father?'" What is the meaning of this speech? Why did Umar say this speech? You notice the hadiths are cut up. The speech will come to us. Read the hadith and judge your conscience. From Abu Huraira, he said: "The Messenger of Allah came out and he was angry, his face red, until he sat on the pulpit. So a man stood to him and said: 'Where is my father?'" What is the meaning of this speech? The Prophet is angry, sits on the pulpit, a person stands up and says 'Where is my father'? "He said: 'In the Fire.' So another stood up and said: 'Who is my father?' He said: 'Your father is Hudhafah.' So Umar ibn al-Khattab said..." You notice the questions and the speech, there is no connection between them.

So Umar ibn al-Khattab stood up and said... this is a speech of apology! 'We are pleased with Allah as a Lord, and with Islam as a religion, and with Muhammad as a prophet, and with the Quran as an Imam. Indeed we, O Messenger of Allah, are newly out of ignorance and polytheism, and Allah knows who our fathers are!'

Therefore Umar here is the one who apologizes! Until you notice the extent of the distortion in the books of the people. Therefore Umar here is the one who stood up: "We are pleased with Allah as a Lord, and with Islam as a religion, and with Muhammad as a prophet, and with the Quran as an Imam. Indeed we, O Messenger of Allah, are newly out of ignorance and polytheism"—meaning known lineages! "And Allah knows who our fathers are!" He said: "So his anger subsided, and the verse descended: 'O you who have believed, do not ask about things which, if they are shown to you, will distress you.'" This is an arrangement, of course. The verse was arranged with this incident. After that, they arranged another arrangement for the verse, so they said that this verse descended when the verse descended: "And due to Allah from the people is a pilgrimage to the House - for whoever is able to find thereto a way." "They said: 'O Messenger of Allah, is it every year?' So he remained silent. Then they said: 'Is it every year?' So he remained silent. Then he said: 'No, and if I said yes, it would have become obligatory.' So Allah revealed this verse: 'O you who have believed, do not ask about things which, if they are shown to you, will distress you.'" What is the connection of this verse with this verse? After it is **Tafsir Ibn Kathir**. And this is the third and fourth volume, this edition is the Al-Maktabah Al-Tawfiqiyyah edition. This is the third volume, page 125, in the tail of the verse: "O you who have believed, do not ask about things which, if they are shown to you, will distress you," to the end of the noble verse. On page 127, with his chain of narration, from al-Suddi, that he said regarding the saying of the Exalted: "O you who have believed, do not ask about things which, if they are shown to you, will distress you." He said: "The Messenger of Allah became angry one day, so he stood preaching." Why did the Messenger of Allah become angry one day and stand preaching? "He said: 'Ask me! For you will not ask me about a thing except I will inform you of it.' And he said: 'Ask me about your fathers, about your lineages.' When you say..." but the hadiths are arranged! "So a man from Quraish, from Bani Sahm, called Abdullah ibn Hudhafah stood up to him—and he used to be maligned regarding it, maligned regarding his lineage—so he said: 'O Messenger of Allah, who is my father?' So he said: 'Your father is so-and-so.' So he attributed him to his father.

So Umar ibn al-Khattab stood up to him, and kissed his foot! The foot of the Prophet! And said: 'O Messenger of Allah, we are pleased with Allah as a Lord, and with you as a prophet, and with Islam as a religion, and with the Quran as an Imam. So pardon us, may Allah pardon you.' He kept insisting on him until he was pleased! On that day, the Prophet said: 'The child belongs to the bed, and for the adulterer is the stone.'

You notice what the atmosphere is! The atmosphere is the talk about lineage, the father, the son to his father. Because they said, and this saying was said by Umar, that Muhammad is a palm tree that grew in a Kiba, in a sweepings, in a garbage dump! Therefore Umar here apologizes. And the hadiths are mutilated. We will come to the hadith in its complete form from its correct source. I read the narration a second time. This is Tafsir Ibn Kathir, and this is the third volume, page 127. And as I said, this is the Al-Maktabah Al-Tawfiqiyyah edition. "Asbat, from al-Suddi, that he said regarding the saying of the Exalted: 'O you who have believed, do not ask about things which, if they are shown to you, will distress you.' He said: The Messenger of Allah became angry one day. Why? Does he become angry without reason? So he stood preaching. He said: 'Ask me! For you will not ask me about a thing...' not about absolute things! Here they distorted the speech. He said: 'Ask me about your lineages'... 'except I will inform you of it.' So a man from Quraish stood up to him..." And this is the reason. You notice how the report is chopped up between the books: between al-Tabari, between al-Bukhari, between Ibn Kathir! "So a man from Quraish, from Bani Sahm, called Abdullah ibn Hudhafah stood up to him, and he used to be maligned regarding it, in his lineage. So he said: 'O Messenger of Allah, who is my father?' So he said: 'Your father is so-and-so.' His father is like so-and-so. So he attributed him to his father. Meaning he said that you are the son of your father.

**So Umar ibn al-Khattab stood up to him immediately! Because he was certain that the Prophet would come to the mention of Umar ibn al-Khattab, and would demand of him, 'Stand and ask me about your father!' Because he is the one who said about the Messenger of Allah that he is a palm tree that grew in a sweeping place!** "So Umar ibn al-Khattab stood up to him, and kissed his foot! The foot of the Prophet. And said: 'O Messenger of Allah, we are pleased with Allah as a Lord, and with you as a prophet, and with Islam as a religion, and with the Quran as an Imam. So pardon us, may Allah pardon you.' He kept persisting with him until he was pleased. On that day, he said: 'The child belongs to the bed, and for the adulterer is the stone.'" This is Tafsir Ibn Kathir.

This is **Al-Durr al-Manthur**, and I take the sources according to history, considering al-Tabari is older than Ibn Kathir, and Ibn Kathir is older than al-Suyuti. This is Al-Durr al-Manthur, the third volume, Dar Ihya' al-Turath al-Arabi also, in the tail of verse 101 from Surah al-Ma'idah, "O you who have believed, do not ask about things which, if they are shown to you, will distress you," page 188. The exact same hadith.

And Umar stands up and kisses the foot of the Prophet, and says: 'O Messenger of Allah, we are pleased with Allah as a Lord, and with you as a prophet, and with the Quran as an Imam. So pardon us, may Allah pardon you.' He kept persisting with him until he was pleased. On that day, he said: 'The child belongs to the bed, and for the adulterer is the stone.' And he also brings the hadith that came narrated from Abu Huraira, until "Umar stood up and said: 'We are pleased with Allah as a Lord, and with Islam as a religion, and with Muhammad as a prophet, and with the Quran as an Imam. Indeed we, O Messenger of Allah, are newly out of ignorance and polytheism, and Allah knows best who our fathers are.' So his anger subsided," the anger of the Prophet subsided, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family.

You notice the hadiths are cut up! But if we wanted to search in their corners, it is possible for us to discover something of the truth. But the complete truth is here! The complete truth is in this book. This is the **book of Sulaym ibn Qays**, the ABCs of the Shia, as our Imam al-Sadiq says. "Whoever does not have in his house the book of Sulaym ibn Qays, then he does not have anything of the secrets of the family of Muhammad." The intended by secrets is the secrets of the first events that occurred, the events of the first sedition (Fitnah). The hadith is long, but I will suffice with the point of need. Page 684. This is the second volume of the book verified with the verification of Sheikh Muhammad Baqir al-Ansari, page 684 and what follows it.
Ali, peace be upon him, said: Then I passed by the Al-Sahhaki one day...
The Al-Sahhaki is Umar ibn al-Khattab, attributed to his grandmother Sahhak.
Ali, peace be upon him, said: Then I passed by the Al-Sahhaki one day, so he said to me: 'The likeness of Muhammad is nothing but like the likeness of a palm tree that grew in a dunghill (Kunasah)!'

This is the saying of Umar! "Then I passed by the Al-Sahhaki one day, so he said to me: 'The likeness of Muhammad is nothing but like the likeness of a palm tree that grew in a dunghill!' Out of hatred from him, and dispraise, and enmity to the family of the house of the Prophet.
'So I came to the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, and mentioned that to him. So the Prophet became angry and went out in anger!'

This is the anger whose reason was not clarified! "So the Prophet became angry and went out in anger! He came to the pulpit, and the Ansar became alarmed." In the story of Umar, he pointed to the going out of the Ansar! You notice the truths are scattered here and there and hidden. The truth was buried. But the truth is here, this speech is the speech of Ali. And the truth is with Ali, it turns with him wherever he turns. This is the reason that makes us love Ali! Because Ali does not deceive. Because Ali does not lie, as he said about himself, may prayers of Allah be upon him, and upon the Messenger of Allah, and upon their pure family. As he said: "And he did not find in me a lie in speech." Meaning the Prophet did not find in Ali a lie in speech; others are liars! "And he did not find in me a lie in speech, nor a mistake in action."

"Ali, peace be upon him, said: Then I passed by the Al-Sahhaki one day, so he said to me: 'The likeness of Muhammad is nothing but like the likeness of a palm tree that grew in a dunghill!' So I came to the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, and mentioned that to him. So the Prophet became angry and went out in anger! He came to the pulpit, and the Ansar became alarmed, so they came fully armed in weapons!" Fully armed, meaning the weapons covered them. "When they saw the anger of the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family.

So he said: 'What is the matter with people who taunt me with my relatives? While they have heard from me what I said regarding their virtue and Allah's preferring them! And what Allah has uniquely favored them with of removing impurity from them!'

The talk is not about al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib! The talk is about the people of the Verse of Purification (Ahlulbayt). This whole speech is for the sake of Ali! Against Ali, this whole speech is against Ali! "While they have heard from me what I said regarding their virtue and Allah's preferring them! And what Allah has uniquely favored them with of removing impurity from them, and Allah's purifying them!
And you have heard what I said regarding the best of my Ahlulbayt!'

Who is it? It is Ali. "And you have heard what I said regarding the best of my Ahlulbayt!" Notice the whole speech is against Ali, and therefore the Prophet points to this truth. "'And you have heard what I said regarding the best of my Ahlulbayt! And the finest of them due to what Allah uniquely favored him with, and honored him, and preferred him of his precedence in Islam, and his trial in it, and his kinship to me, and that he is to me in the position of Aaron to Moses.
Then you claim that my likeness among my Ahlulbayt is like the likeness of a palm tree that grew in a dunghill?!

"'Indeed, Allah created His creation and divided them into two groups, so He placed me in the best of the two groups. Then He divided the group into three groups: peoples, tribes, and houses. And He placed me in its best people, and its best tribe. Then He made them houses, so He placed me in its best house! So that is His saying: Allah intends only to remove from you the impurity, Ahlulbayt, and to purify you with [extensive] purification.
So it (the purity) was confined to my Ahlulbayt and my progeny: me, and my brother Ali ibn Abi Talib!'

The pivot here is Ali ibn Abi Talib. Therefore he did not mention the names of his Ahlulbayt, he mentioned the name of Ali! "'So it was confined to my Ahlulbayt and my progeny: me, and my brother Ali ibn Abi Talib.'" From here, when we raise Ali as a slogan, because Muhammad raised him as a slogan! Not as the opponents want to, or babble, that we prefer Ali over Muhammad. This is babbling. We follow Muhammad because we annihilate ourselves in the obedience of Muhammad! We raise Ali as a slogan because we are certain that Muhammad loves this. He said: "The mention of Ali is worship." Therefore we constantly utter his name, because he is the one who commanded us with that. He said: "His mention is worship, mention him." He said: "Adorn your gatherings with the mention of Ali." So the mention of Ali is worship! We adorn our hearts, we adorn our minds, we adorn our satellite channels, we adorn our homes, our gatherings, we adorn our books, our hadiths, we adorn everything in our lives! We adorn life with the mention of Ali, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him.

"'Indeed, Allah looked at the people of the earth a look...'" The speech of Muhammad is still continuing, this is the sermon of Muhammad when he went out angry! This is the authentic speech, not these cut-up, forged books! "'Indeed, Allah looked at the people of the earth a look, so He chose me from them. Then He looked a look, so He chose my brother Ali, and my vizier, and my legatee!'" And these are his descriptions since the verse descended: "And warn your closest kindred." "'Then He looked a look, so He chose my brother Ali, and my vizier, and my legatee, and my successor in my nation, and the master of every believer after me. So He sent me as a messenger, and a prophet, and a guide. And He revealed to me to take Ali as a brother, and a master, and a legatee, and a successor in my nation after me.'" Until he says—and he delivers a long sermon, I take clips from it: "'And the enemies of Allah want to extinguish the light of my brother, but Allah refuses except to perfect His light!'" Then he talks about the Twelve Imams and details the speech regarding his pure progeny. The sermon is long, review it, pages 684, 685, 686.

"'Indeed, I am Muhammad ibn Abdullah, I am the best of the messengers and prophets. And Fatima my daughter is the mistress of the women of the people of Paradise. And Ali and his sons the Legatees are the best of legatees. And my Ahlulbayt are the best of the people of the houses of the prophets. And my two sons are the two masters of the youth of the people of Paradise.'" Until he says:

'O people! Trace my lineage! Who am I?' So a man from the Ansar stood up to him and said: 'We seek refuge in Allah from the anger of Allah and from the anger of His Messenger! Inform us, O Messenger of Allah, who is the one who harmed you regarding your Ahlulbayt so we may strike his neck?' He said: 'Trace my lineage. I am Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib ibn Hashim, until I trace back to Nizar.'

This is his lineage. He said: "'Trace my lineage.'" Then he said: "'I am Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib ibn Hashim, until I trace back to Nizar.' Then he proceeded in his lineage to Ismail ibn Ibrahim the Friend of Allah. Then he proceeded in his lineage to Ismail ibn Ibrahim the Friend of Allah. Then he said: 'I and my Ahlulbayt are of a pure clay from beneath the Throne to Adam! A marriage, not fornication, the marriage of the pre-Islamic era never mixed with it! So ask me! So ask me! So ask me! For by Allah, no man will ask me about his father, and about his mother, and about his lineage, except I will inform him of it!'" This speech which they said "ask me about anything," he talked about this speech! "'So ask me! For by Allah, no man will ask me about his father, and about his mother...'" And the lineage of Umar is known in the books, I do not want to talk about it.

"So a man stood up to him..." "'So ask me! For by Allah, no man will ask me about his father, and about his mother, and about his lineage, except I will inform him of it!' So a man stood up to him and said: 'Who is my father?' So he, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, said: 'Your father is so-and-so to whom you are attributed.' So he praised Allah and extolled Him, and said: 'If you had attributed me to other than him, O Messenger of Allah, I would have been pleased and submitted.' Then another man stood up, then another man asked, and another asked, and other questions. Until the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, said while he was angry:

'What prevents the one who taunted the best of my Ahlulbayt, and my brother, and my vizier, and my heir, and my legatee, and my successor in my nation, and the master of every believer after me, from standing up and asking me who his father is?! And where is he, is he in Paradise or in the Fire?'
Where is he, meaning the son, is he in Paradise or in the Fire, or about the father.

Then the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, said while he was angry... the beginning of the speech, the Commander of the Believers said: "I passed by the Al-Sahhaki one day, so he said to me: The likeness of Muhammad is nothing but like a palm tree that grew in a dunghill!" And here he alludes to Ali, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. Then he said: "Then the Messenger of Allah said while he was angry: 'What prevents the one who taunted the best of my Ahlulbayt, and my brother, and my vizier, and my heir, and my legatee, and my successor in my nation, and the master of every believer after me, from standing up and asking me who his father is?! And where is he, is he in Paradise or in the Fire?'

So Umar ibn al-Khattab stood up to him and said: 'I seek refuge in Allah from the wrath of Allah and the wrath of His Messenger! Pardon us, O Messenger of Allah, may Allah pardon you! Discharge us, may Allah discharge you! Cover us, may Allah cover you! Forgive us, may Allah bless you!' So the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, felt shy and stopped!"

You notice where the complete story is? It is with Ali! When we say the perfect religion: "Today I have perfected for you your religion." The complete truth is with the Pure Progeny! O people, we have no interest in talking about the likes of these hadiths except uncovering the truths! We do not have a personal problem with a person. O people, O Muslims, O you who love Muhammad, these are the truths! Judge your consciences. These are your books and these are our books. Gather the truths, scrutinize your look into them. This issue alone is enough to make a human ride in the Ship of Salvation! To see how much is the distortion, and how much is the forgery, and where the complete truth is. I ask you by Allah, review the texts which I mentioned in these books, and review this complete text, to know where the truth is, and who is the one who said about Muhammad that he is a plant that grew in a garbage dump, and to whom this speech was directed, and why Umar was asking for pardon in those texts and kissing the foot of the Prophet and persisting with him! The picture is not clear there; the clear, complete picture is here.

This is the etiquette of Umar with the Messenger of Allah. And these are truths. And when I say truths are buried, when the talk is about Ali, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. Therefore I said at the beginning of my speech, since the first episode: this program, the Alawi File, and these episodes, I do not talk in them about the oppression of Ali, for I would be wronging it! The issue is greater than seven episodes and a hundred episodes! A great oppression. By Allah, the oppression of Ali is in every line of the lines of these books. In every word of the words of these books there is an oppression of Ali! So what oppression can I talk about in a limited number of hours? The oppression of Ali is in every letter, in every minute of the minutes of the lives of the tyrants who ruled the nation since the day of Saqifah and up to this day, there is an oppression of Ali!

We too oppress Ali, because we do not give Ali his due. We too oppress Ali, but an oppression from another aspect. The oppression of Ali lives among us. If we want to support Ali, to revive the affair of Ali—and reviving the affair of Ali is reviving the affair of the Master of the Affair (Al-Mahdi). Reviving the affair of Ali is spreading the thought of Ali as much as we can. Reviving the affair of Ali is reviving the affair of Hussein. And Hussein is approaching at the doors, the days of Hussein are at the doors. Reviving the affair of Ali is reviving the affair of Hussein, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him. And the talk brings up sorrows, and the speech is long, and the truths are many, and I do not know from where to begin and where to end!
Segment 9: The Touchstone of Lineage and the Ship of Salvation

Sheikh al-Ghizzi concludes Episode 6 by reminding viewers that true salvation lies in boarding the "Ship of Salvation" of the Ahlulbayt, urging believers to strive for a "first-class seat" through genuine devotion and love for Imam Ali. He beautifully quotes the famous poetry of Safi al-Din al-Hilli, which declares that Imam Ali is the ultimate touchstone for legitimate birth: mentioning Ali’s name brings joy and purity to those of noble lineage, while it provokes immediate malice and anger in those of illegitimate birth.

Salvation is only that we ride in a ship of salvation. And we, praise be to Allah, O lovers of Ali—you and we, and everyone who loves Ali—we have all ridden in the ship of salvation. But "the son of his father" (a true man) among us is the one who can preserve his chair in the ship of salvation! The son of his father among us is the one who can preserve this ship and pay its fare; you cannot board a ship without a fare.

The son of his father among us is the one who can secure a chair, a seat, in the first class in this ship! This ship has classes. The son of his father among us is the one who can secure a seat in the first class in this ship, in the best positions in it. The son of his father among us is the one who can pay the fare of this ship:
Say, I do not ask of you for it any reward except love for the near of kin.
Love for the near of kin means to be with Ali! It means for Ali to sit squarely upon our hearts, for Ali to sit upon our minds, to sit upon our feelings and our emotions.

"O Commander of the Believers, I see that when I mention you to one of noble lineage, he becomes pure to me." May Allah have mercy on the poet Safi al-Din al-Hilli. "O Commander of the Believers, I see that when I mention you to one of noble lineage, he listens to me (Sagha li)." He listens to me, he becomes pure to me, he opens his heart to me.

"O Commander of the Believers, I see that when I mention you to one of noble lineage, he becomes pure to me. And if I mention you to an illegitimate one (Naghl), his secret becomes disturbed and he seeks to fight me! So I have become such that, if I doubt the origin of a man, I mention you with a beautiful speech. For no one can bear hearing your praise except the one of noble origin and praiseworthy traits. So here I am, I have tested the creations with you. For you, you are the touchstone for the legitimate children (Awlad al-Halal)!"

Our appointment is renewed, God willing, tomorrow, the final episode of the Alawi File. I ask you for supplication. I leave you in the care of the Master of Legatees, Al-Murtada. In the safety of Allah.

title: The File of Ali - Episode 7: The Calamity of Thursday and the Need for an Infallible Guide
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 7

In this final installment of the Alawi File, Sheikh al-Ghizzi delivers a comprehensive analysis of the **Calamity of Thursday**, using **Sahih al-Bukhari** and **Sahih Muslim** to prove that the Prophet was **systematically prevented** from securing the nation's guidance through a written will. He exposes the **intellectual dishonesty** of canonical commentators who attempt to rebrand explicit disobedience as "Ijtihad." The Sheikh contrasts the **documented failure** of the Companions at Khaybar with the hidden virtues of Ali found in Al-Hakim’s **Mustadrak**, arguing that the "Two Sheikhs" (Bukhari and Muslim) participated in a **deliberate burial of truth**. Finally, he employs the **logical proofs** of Hisham ibn al-Hakam and Sayyid Muhammad Baqir al-Sadr to establish that an **Infallible Custodian** is a rational necessity for the Quran to function as a divine proof, concluding that Ali ibn Abi Talib is the **impenetrable fortress** and the only gate to Prophetic knowledge.
Segment 1: The Prevention of the Prophetic Will

Sheikh al-Ghizzi introduces the seventh and final episode of "The File of Ali," outlining three main subjects. He begins by addressing the tragic "Calamity of Thursday." Reciting directly from Sahih al-Bukhari, the Sheikh details how the Prophet, in his final moments, requested a pen and paper to write a document guaranteeing the nation would never go astray. He emphasizes that Umar ibn al-Khattab's refusal—claiming the Prophet was "overcome by pain" (delirious) and stating "the Book of Allah is sufficient for us"—was an act of sheer defiance. The Sheikh asks a vital question: What could this document have contained to permanently prevent misguidance, if not the explicit appointment of a divine measure (Mizan)—Imam Ali—to guide the Ummah?

In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Peace be upon the Radiant Face of Allah and His High Side. Peace be upon the ripe fruit and the brilliant star. Peace be upon the pleasing name of Allah, Abu al-Hassan Ali, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings. Peace be upon you, O followers of Abu Turab, in every corner of the corners of this existence, wherever you are, in every time and place—living and dead, young and old, men and women, elders and youth. Between your hands is the Alawi File, the seventh episode.

The seventh episode is the final episode of this program. And today as well is the final day of the Hijri year 1431, and tomorrow a new Hijri year begins. In this episode of the Alawi File, I will divide my speech into three directions. The first direction: what I pointed to in the past episode regarding the **hadith of the Calamity of Thursday** (Raziyyat Yawm al-Khamis). I stop in this episode at **Sahih al-Bukhari** and **Sahih Muslim** to read what came of the hadith specifically regarding this calamity in these two Sahihs.

This is **Sahih al-Bukhari**, Dar Sader edition. According to this edition, page 34, Hadith 114. With his chain of narration from Ibn Abbas, he said: When the illness of the Prophet became severe, he said:
Bring me a book so I may write for you a document after which you will not go astray.
Umar said:
Indeed the Prophet is overcome by pain, and we have the Book of Allah, it is sufficient for us.
So they differed and the noise increased. He, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, said:
Get up and leave me, for there should be no quarreling in my presence.
So Ibn Abbas went out saying: "The calamity, all the calamity, is what intervened between the Messenger of Allah and his writing of his document!"

The Prophet, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, wants to establish a measure (**Mizan**), a measure to which the nation returns to distinguish guidance from misguidance. **What is this measure?** The Quran? The Quran has clear (Muhkam) verses and ambiguous (Mutashabih) verses; it needs a measure to distinguish the clear from the ambiguous. The Messenger of Allah, in the final moment of the moments of his life, asks for a document to write something in it for the nation so it does not go astray after this writing.

Umar ibn al-Khattab says, "The Book of Allah is sufficient for us." So he is more knowledgeable than the Messenger of Allah! Does the Messenger of Allah not know that the Book of Allah is present with the Muslims? He said: "The Book of Allah is sufficient for us; we are in no need of your document, O Messenger of Allah!" And he said, "He is overcome by pain," and he said, "He is delirious." **Delirious**, meaning this Muhammad is speaking nonsense, but Umar does not speak nonsense! Muhammad, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, speaks nonsense, but Abu Bakr does not speak nonsense!
Segment 2: "Thursday, and What is Thursday!" - The Tears of Ibn Abbas

Sheikh al-Ghizzi meticulously traces the "Calamity of Thursday" across multiple chapters in Sahih al-Bukhari to expose the deliberate tampering and softening of the narrative by Sunni compilers. He highlights how Ibn Abbas wept so profusely over this event that his tears soaked the pebbles. In different variations of the hadith, the Companions explicitly state that the Prophet is "delirious" (Hajara), which al-Bukhari sometimes tries to disguise as a question ("Is he delirious?"). The Sheikh mocks the absurdity of later Sunni justifications, which claim the Prophet abandoned the eternal document of guidance merely to give three standard administrative commands (one of which the narrator conveniently "forgot"). Furthermore, he exposes the irony of Umar claiming "the Book of Allah is sufficient" when Umar's gross ignorance of basic Quranic laws is widely documented.

This is page 34, Hadith 114. And likewise on page 541 of **Sahih al-Bukhari**. From Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas, may Allah be pleased with them both, that he said: **"Thursday, and what is Thursday!"** Then he wept until his tears dyed the pebbles! So he said: "The illness of the Messenger of Allah became severe on Thursday, so he said:
Bring me a book so I may write for you a document, you will never go astray after it, ever!

**Lan** (never) is for eternal negation. And Muhammad is the most eloquent of those who spoke with the letter **Daad** (the Arabic language). "You will never go astray after it, ever!" He used **Lan** for eternal negation, and restricted it with an additional restriction: "ever" (Abadan). "Bring me a book so I may write for you a document, you will never go astray after it, ever!"

So they disputed, and there should be no dispute in the presence of a prophet. So they said: "The Messenger of Allah is **delirious (Hajara)!**" They said! Who are the ones who said? Upon the method of al-Bukhari, he loses the facts: "So they said." The result is that the Companions said "he is delirious." Meaning this Messenger of Allah is speaking nonsense!

The strange thing is that I know that many of them, when they hear this speech of mine, will not become angry for the Messenger of Allah, but they will become angry for Umar ibn al-Khattab! And here faith is distinguished from disbelief, and guidance from misguidance. We are with the Messenger of Allah, and we are not with anyone else! **If we are with Ali, it is because Ali is with the Messenger of Allah.**

On page 777, in the Chapter of the Illness of the Prophet and his Death. From Sa'id ibn Jubayr, he said: "Ibn Abbas said: Thursday, and what is Thursday! The illness of the Messenger of Allah became severe, so he said: 'Bring to me, I will write for you a document, you will never go astray after it, ever.' So they disputed... So they said: 'What is his matter? Is he delirious? Ask him to explain!' **So they went replying back to him!** Replying back to the speech of the Prophet!

Ubaydullah said: "So Ibn Abbas used to say: 'The calamity, all the calamity, is what intervened between the Messenger of Allah and his writing of that document for them, from their differing and their noise!'"

The Prophet wanted to clarify a safety valve for this nation, to give this nation a safety valve. He laid down a methodology: **"The Book of Allah is sufficient for us."** And would that Umar ibn al-Khattab knew what is in the Book of Allah! The witnesses and hadiths passed upon us from the books of the people that he was ignorant of the rulings of prayer, ignorant of the rulings of Tayammum, ignorant of the rulings of recitation, ignorant of the rulings of penal codes (Hudud).
Segment 3: Sahih Muslim and the Mental Gymnastics of the Commentators

Sheikh al-Ghizzi reads the "Calamity of Thursday" from Sahih Muslim, which mirrors al-Bukhari but confirms the Companions explicitly accused the Prophet of being "delirious" (yahjur). The Sheikh then meticulously dismantles the mental gymnastics used by major Sunni commentators, specifically Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani in Fath al-Bari and Al-Qurtubi. These commentators absurdly attempt to justify Umar's disobedience by claiming it was a brilliant "Ijtihad" (independent reasoning) to protect the "virtue of scholars" and that the Prophet's command was merely a suggestion. The Sheikh exposes the utter fallacy of claiming "Ijtihad" in the direct presence of the Prophet, comparing it to Umar's "Ijtihad" in ignoring explicit Quranic verses regarding Tayammum.

I go to **Sahih Muslim**. From al-Aswad ibn Yazid, he said: "They mentioned in the presence of Aisha that Ali was a legatee. So she said: 'When did he make a will to him?'" ... Page 619: "The illness of the Messenger of Allah became severe, so he said: 'Bring to me, I will write for you a document, you will not go astray after me.' So they disputed... And they said: 'What is his matter? Is he delirious? Ask him to explain!'

I would have liked to read what the commentators of hadiths wrote in detail... This is the book **Fath al-Bari** by one of their known scholars, **Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani**. He says: "Al-Qurtubi and others said: 'Bring me' is a command, and it was the duty of the commanded to hasten to comply. But it appeared to Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, along with a group, that it was not for obligation! And that it was from the category of guidance to what is best, so they disliked to burden him!"

By Allah, does the one who reads the hadiths understand this from them? "And this was counted as from the agreements (**Muwafaqat**) of Umar, may Allah be pleased with him!" Meaning this is a miracle (Karamah) for Umar! Therefore the Prophet did not return and write the book! "Umar went to the view that if he had explicitly stated what removes the difference, **the virtue of the scholars and of Ijtihad (independent reasoning) would have been invalidated!**"

Meaning that Umar undertook this matter for what reason? To preserve the virtue of the scholars and the virtue of Ijtihad! **What Ijtihad is this? Ijtihad in the face of the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family?!**
Segment 4: The Hadiths of the Pond (Hawdh) and the Apostasy of the Companions

Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents a terrifying series of hadiths from Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim known as the "Hadiths of the Pond." These narrations explicitly state that on the Day of Judgment, the vast majority of the Prophet's Companions will be driven away from the Pond of Kawthar and cast into the Fire because they "turned back on their heels" (apostatized) after his death. The Prophet declares that only a tiny fraction will be saved, comparing them to "stray cattle." The Sheikh then exposes the blatant dishonesty of Sunni commentators like al-Nawawi, who absurdly try to claim these condemned Companions are actually the "Kharijites and Rawafidh" (Shias), completely ignoring the Prophet's explicit use of the word "my companions." He contrasts this with a profound hadith from Al-Kafi illustrating that a Muslim without a divinely appointed Imam is like a lost sheep devoured by wolves.

There are other narrations I will recite to your hearing from **Sahih al-Bukhari** and from **Sahih Muslim** too. Book of Tribulations (Fitn). From Abu Hazim, he said: I heard Sahl ibn Sa'd saying: I heard the Prophet saying:

I am your predecessor at the Pond. Whoever arrives at it will drink from it, and whoever drinks from it will never thirst after it, ever. Peoples will certainly come to me whom I know and who know me! Then a barrier will be placed between me and them... So I will say: 'Away, away (Suhqan Suhqa) with those who changed after me!'

From Abu Huraira, Hadith 6585: "A group of my companions will come to me on the Day of Resurrection, and they will be driven away from the Pond! ... So He will say: **'You have no knowledge of what they innovated after you. Indeed, they apostatized on their backs backward (Irtaddu ala Adbarihim Qahqara)!'**"

Then what does the Prophet say? **"So I do not see any of them saved except like stray cattle (Hamal al-Na'am)!"** Stray cattle, meaning the lost, neglected animals. Meaning those who will be saved from the companions of the Prophet on the Day of Judgment, their number is like the number of animals that stray and wander away from their owners! A very small number!
I remind you of what I read to you from **Al-Kafi** in the hadith of the stray, wandering sheep. From Muhammad ibn Muslim, he said: I heard Abu Ja'far (Imam al-Baqir) saying:

Everyone who is subservient to Allah the Mighty and Majestic with worship in which he exerts himself, but he has no Imam from Allah, then his effort is unaccepted, and he is lost and bewildered... while it was in that state, a wolf took advantage of its loss and ate it!
This is the stray sheep. This is the stray camel. "I will surely drive men away from my Pond just as the strange camel is driven away from the camels."
Segment 5: The "Crime" of Al-Hakim al-Nayshaburi

Sheikh al-Ghizzi shifts to examining Al-Mustadrak ala al-Sahihayn by Al-Hakim al-Nayshaburi, a highly respected Sunni hadith scholar. The Sheikh explains that al-Nayshaburi's stated goal was to compile authentic hadiths that met the strict criteria of Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim but were mysteriously excluded by them. The Sheikh proceeds to read numerous explicitly authentic hadiths from the Mustadrak—including the Hadith of the Ship, the flight of Abu Bakr and Umar at Khaybar, and the Hadith of Ghadir—which prove Imam Ali's supreme rank. He points out that al-Nayshaburi's "crime" in the eyes of later Sunni scholars was simply recording these pro-Ali hadiths, leading them to falsely accuse him of Shiite leanings (Tashayyu') to discredit his work.

A very clear example of the burying of the truth is this book: **Al-Mustadrak ala al-Sahihayn** by **Al-Hakim al-Nayshaburi**. The title Al-Hakim is an academic degree, meaning he is at a level that allows him to judge hadiths and the men of hadith. In the beginning of the book, he explains his intention:
I seek Allah's help in extracting hadiths in this book whose narrators are trustworthy, which the Two Sheikhs—may Allah be pleased with them both—or one of them, used as proof.

The Two Sheikhs, meaning al-Bukhari and Muslim. Meaning he is gathering in this book the hadiths that fit the rules of Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim, but al-Bukhari and Muslim did not mention these hadiths! Why did al-Hakim al-Nayshaburi become a criminal in their eyes? **The crime of al-Hakim al-Nayshaburi is as follows:**

Hadith 3365: I heard Abu Dharr saying while he was holding the door of the Kaaba: "I heard the Messenger of Allah saying: **'The likeness of my Ahlulbayt is like the Ship of Nuh. Whoever rides it is saved, and whoever stays behind it drowns!'**" Al-Hakim says: "This is an authentic hadith upon the condition of Muslim, and they did not extract it."

Hadith 4395: "Indeed the Messenger of Allah sent Abu Bakr to Khaybar, so he marched with the people, and he was defeated until he returned!" "This hadith has an authentic chain of narration, and they did not extract it."

Hadith 4397: "Umar and his companions were defeated, so they came back making Umar a coward, and him making them cowards!" "This hadith has an authentic chain of narration, and they did not extract it."

Muawiyah said to Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas: "What prevents you from cursing Ibn Abi Talib?" ... Al-Hakim al-Nayshaburi says: "This hadith is authentic upon the condition of the Two Sheikhs, and they did not extract it in this context." Rather, they distorted it into another form. **There is always a hiding of Ali!**
Segment 6: The Hidden Virtues and the Prophetic Declaration

Sheikh al-Ghizzi continues to cite authentic narrations from al-Hakim's Mustadrak that were suppressed by al-Bukhari and Muslim despite meeting their criteria. These include the unique virtues of Imam Ali as the first to pray with the Prophet and the only one whose house remained open to the mosque. Most critically, he highlights a hadith from Anas ibn Malik stating that the Prophet declared Ali to be the one who would "clarify for the nation what they differ in" after him—a direct link to the prevented document of the "Calamity of Thursday." The Sheikh argues that the deliberate omission of these hadiths by the "Two Sheikhs" (al-Bukhari and Muslim) is the ultimate evidence of their role in burying the truth of Ali's successorship.

On page 918, Hadith 4639: "Ali has four traits that no one else has. He is the first Arab and non-Arab to pray with the Messenger of Allah. He is the one whose banner was with him in every march." These are authentic hadiths upon the condition of the Two Sheikhs, but the Two Sheikhs did not mention these hadiths.

Hadith 4672: That he [the Prophet] said to Ali: "You are from me and I am from you." Al-Hakim comments: "This hadith has an authentic chain of narration, and they did not extract it with these words." They changed the words. **You notice, they also know this truth!**
Hadith 4673: I heard the Messenger of Allah saying: **"Whoever insults Ali has insulted me."** He says: "This hadith has an authentic chain of narration, and they did not extract it."

Hadith 4678: From Anas ibn Malik, that the Prophet said to Ali: **"You shall clarify to my nation what they differ in after me."** This is what he wanted to write in the document so the nation would not go astray! And he comments: "This is an authentic hadith upon the condition of the Two Sheikhs, and they did not extract it." This is the crime of al-Nayshaburi, and this is the virtue of al-Bukhari and Muslim, O Muslim!

Hadith 4685: I heard the Messenger of Allah saying: **"Ali is with the Quran and the Quran is with Ali; they shall never separate until they arrive at the Pond."** This hadith has an authentic chain of narration, and they did not extract it.
Segment 7: The Closing of the Doors and the Unique Inheritance of Ali

Sheikh al-Ghizzi continues to explore the authentic hadiths documented in Al-Hakim’s Mustadrak that expose the unique spiritual and political status of Imam Ali. He focuses on the "Hadith of the Doors," where the Prophet commanded all doors leading to the mosque to be sealed except for Ali’s, and the "Hadith of the Three Qualities," where Umar himself admits that Ali was granted three honors more precious than "red camels." Most notably, he presents the "Hadith of the City of Knowledge," establishing Ali as the sole gateway to Prophetic wisdom. He argues that the omission of these narrations from the two Sahihs is not a matter of technical authenticity, but a deliberate political act to hide the "Safety Valve" of the Ummah.

Hadith 4688: From Zayd ibn Arqam, he said: "A group from the companions of the Messenger of Allah had doors opening into the mosque. So the Prophet said one day: **'Close all these doors except for the door of Ali.'**" He says: "This hadith has an authentic chain of narration, and they did not extract it."

Hadith 4689: From Abu Huraira, he said: Umar ibn al-Khattab said: **"Indeed, Ali ibn Abi Talib was given three qualities; if I had even one of them, it would be more beloved to me than being given red camels."** His marriage to Fatima, his residing in the mosque where what is lawful for the Prophet is lawful for him, and the banner on the Day of Khaybar. Abu Bakr proposed to Fatima, and Umar proposed to Fatima, so why did the Greatest Prophet not marry Fatima except to Ali? They were not peers for Fatima; Ali was the only peer for Fatima.

Hadith 4693: The Messenger of Allah said: **"I am the City of Knowledge and Ali is its Gate. So whoever desires the city, let him come to the gate!"** It is not walls, partitions, or windows; it is one gate! Al-Hakim says: "This hadith has an authentic chain of narration, and they did not extract it."

Hadith 4697: From Zayd ibn Arqam: "Whoever desires to live my life, and die my death... let him take Ali ibn Abi Talib as a master (**fal-yatawalla**)! For indeed, he shall never take you out of guidance, and he shall never enter you into misguidance!" And that is what he wanted to write in the document such that the nation would not go astray! "This hadith has an authentic chain of narration upon their condition, and they did not extract it."

The problem is Ali, prayers of Allah be upon him. **Our problem is Ali.** All these are authentic hadiths! The reason for omission is not in the chain of narration, nor is the reason in the text—the words are clear. The reason is Ali!
Segment 8: The Logical Proof of Hisham ibn al-Hakam and the Three States

Sheikh al-Ghizzi presents the logical necessity of a divinely appointed guide using the famous argumentation of Hisham ibn al-Hakam. He asserts that after the Prophet, the state of humanity must fall into one of three possibilities: either Allah removed religious obligations (which is false), humans became as knowledgeable as the Prophet (which is impossible), or a divinely appointed, infallible Imam must exist to serve as the ultimate reference for truth. The Sheikh further supports this with a modern philosophical breakdown by Martyr Muhammad Baqir al-Sadr, concluding that a rational Creator would never leave His religion to chance or chaotic "consultation" (Shura), but must prepare a leadership that is a direct extension of the Prophet's mission.

I said that my speech in this episode falls into three directions. The first was in Sahih al-Bukhari and Muslim. The second was in Mustadrak al-Sahihayn by al-Hakim. In the **third direction**, I point to Hisham ibn al-Hakam. He said: "The people after the Messenger of Allah, we cannot imagine their state except in three states."

**The first state:** either it is that Allah removed the religious obligation (**al-Taklif**) from the creation after the Messenger... while the fact is that the nation did not have the obligation removed from it! The lawful of Muhammad is lawful until the Day of Resurrection.

**The second state:** that the people who are obligated became, after the Messenger, scholars at the likes of the level of the Messenger in knowledge! Such that no one needs anyone! Does anyone say that? No one says that!

**The third face:** they must have a Knowledge (a person of knowledge) which the Messenger establishes for them, who does not forget, nor err, nor deviate. Infallible from sins, cleared of mistakes. They need him, and he does not need anyone!

Upon this same pattern, there is a very beautiful research by **Sayyid Muhammad Baqir al-Sadr**. He said: "It is three probabilities... Either the Leader-Messenger stands toward the future of the call with a passive position and leaves it to circumstances and coincidences! (Impossible). Or the Messenger plans for the future and connects the leadership to the nation [Shura]—but we have no clear system for Shura. **There remains the third case:** that the Prophet chooses by the command of Allah a person... such that he becomes a direct extension of the Messenger in his mission. And that is what happened."

So the intellect, the conscience, the logic, and the primordial nature (**Fitrah**) judge the existence of a person specified by the Messenger of Allah. And this is what is supported by the hadiths of the Book and the Progeny.
Segment 9: The Custodian of the Quran and the Incident of al-Kindi

Sheikh al-Ghizzi argues that the Quran, as a book of multiple facets (hammalun dhu wujuh), cannot serve as a proof (hujjah) without a living, divinely appointed Custodian (Qayyim). Citing Al-Kafi, he recounts the debate of Mansur ibn Hazim, who logically proved that Imam Ali is the only one who fits the criteria of this Custodian. The Sheikh then highlights the incident of the philosopher Abu Ishaq al-Kindi, who attempted to write a book on the "contradictions of the Quran" until Imam al-Askari sent a student to dismantle his premises. This segment establishes that without the Ahlulbayt, the Quran is subject to subjective and contradictory human interpretations.
How do we understand the Quran? There is a beautiful hadith in **Al-Kafi** from Mansur ibn Hazim. He said:

I looked into the Quran, and behold, it is used for disputation by the Murji'ite, the Qadarite, and the heretic... Because the Quran is multi-faceted (**Hammalun dhu Wujuh**). So I realized that the Quran cannot be a Proof except with a Custodian (**Qayyim**)! There must be a Custodian... I did not find anyone said to know all of it except Ali, peace be upon him.

The Quran itself tells us in Surah Al Imran: "He who has sent down to you the Book; in it are verses [that are] precise... and others unspecific... no one knows its [true] interpretation except Allah." They placed a stop sign here! To isolate "and those firm in knowledge." This is also from the burying of the truth, but by way of **Tajwid** (rules of recitation)! It should be: **"And no one knows its interpretation except Allah AND those firm in knowledge."**

There is an incident of the philosopher **Abu Ishaq al-Kindi** mentioned in the 50th volume of Bihar al-Anwar. He spent twenty years gathering "contradictions of the Quran." Imam al-Hassan al-Askari sent a student to him with a single question:
If the speaker of this Quran came to you, is it permissible that his intended meaning in what he spoke of it be other than the meanings which you have thought you arrived at?

Al-Kindi realized that logically, there exists a probability that the speaker intended another meaning. He realized the likes of this could only have come out from that House [Ahlulbayt], and he called for fire and burned all that he had authored. Even if we rely on language, who said that these rules are what the Speaker of the Quran intended? **Therefore, there must be a search for a side or a gate that guides us to the intent of the true Speaker.**
Segment 10: The Fortress of Ali and the Final Conclusion

Sheikh al-Ghizzi concludes the seventh and final episode of "The File of Ali" by identifying the Guardianship (Wilayah) of Imam Ali as the ultimate fortress (Hisn) against divine punishment and misguidance. He quotes the famous Hadith al-Silsilah from Imam al-Rida, declaring that entering Ali's fortress provides absolute security in this world and the Hereafter. He ends the series by acknowledging that the "File of Ali" is vast beyond any program's capacity and that the true tragedy lies in the systematic oppression and erasure of Ali's rights throughout history. Finally, he invites all seekers of truth to board the Ship of Hussein as the new Hijri year begins.

And this is the meaning of the Ridawi hadith, the **Hadith al-Silsilah**: "The Guardianship (**Wilayah**) of Ali ibn Abi Talib is my fortress (**Hisni**); so whoever enters my fortress is safe from my punishment." And we are entering into your fortress, O Commander of the Believers!

Whoever wants to be safe from the punishment—from the punishment of misguidance, from the punishment of ignorance in the world, from the punishment of disbelief, from the punishment of the grave, and from the punishment of the Hereafter—then let him enter into this fortress. Congratulations to those entering into this fortress! And glad tidings for them, and a thousand glad tidings!

This is the conclusion of the speech in the **Alawi File**, and this is the end of the seventh episode, and it is the final episode of this program. God willing, I will meet you in other files. I ask you for supplication, and we are approaching the days of Hussein. I request from my brothers, the servants of Hussein, supplication for me and for all my brothers here. We will meet you, God willing, on other occasions upon the love of Ali and the family of Ali, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon them all. In the safety of Allah.

title: The File of Ashura - Episode 1: The Giant Strategic Project
speaker: Sheikh Abdul-Halim al-Ghizzi

content.text: Overview: Episode 1

In this opening episode of **The File of Ashura**, Sheikh al-Ghizzi redefines the tragedy of Karbala not as a mere historical event, but as a **comprehensive reality** where time, space, doctrine, and emotion merge. He introduces Ashura as a **giant strategic project** designed by the Prophet and executed by Imam Husayn with three distinct objectives: the **short-term objective** of uncovering the Ummah's post-prophetic coup, the **medium-term objective** of preserving the continuity of the Infallible Progeny, and the **long-term objective** of preparing the ground for the global Mahdawi project. Through the lens of **conscious reading**, the Sheikh explores Husayn as the ultimate manifestation of **divine beauty**, asserting that the human heart's reaction to him is an innate spiritual response. The episode concludes with the harrowing account of **Muslim bin Aqeel**, the first martyr of the project, in the streets of Kufa.
Segment 1: Introduction and the Question of Ashura

Sheikh al-Ghizzi begins with a poetic and emotional supplication expressing deep longing and devotion to Imam Husayn. Following this, he introduces the core theme of the lecture series based on questions received from viewers. He distills these inquiries into a single, profound question: "What is Ashura?" He clarifies that Ashura is not merely a historical event but a comprehensive reality where time, space, doctrine, and emotion completely merge.

Movement takes me, O Husayn, far away, so it shines in time and space at times, and sets at other times. And I return on my tracks between the eagerness of longing and the ecstasy of union. So I find you sitting cross-legged in a heart, roaming and playing with you in its pastures, glowing, taking a position on the brink of waiting.

And every atom in my existence speaks with its full mouth. I drank love cup after cup, the drink did not run out nor was my thirst quenched. For our love, for our Husayn, moisten your lips with the sweetness of the remembrance of Muhammad and the family of Muhammad.

In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Peace be upon the observatories of your beginnings, O Husayn. Peace be upon the stations of your holiness. Peace be upon the courtyards of your purity. Peace be upon every lowland and highland that leads me to you, so the light of your splendor and your beauty shines from between the folds of the hearts. Peace be upon the spilled blood. Peace be upon your dyed grey hair. Peace be upon your dust-covered cheek. Peace be upon you, O beloved, O most beloved of all beloveds.
O Husayn, I love you with two loves: the love of passion and a love because you are worthy of that. So the praise is not for me in this or that, but to you belongs the praise in this and that.

A bundle of questions arrived to Al-Mawaddah Satellite Channel via the internet. I gather them into one question. And these gatherings will be an answer to this question in this night and in the coming nights, God willing. A brief question in which I summarize all those questions. The question says: **"What is Ashura?"**

Ashura may be a segment of time, the tenth day of every month. If it is filled with sorrows and pains, it is called Ashura. Ashura may be a segment of time. And it may be a specific event that happens on the earth. And it may be, and it may be, but the Ashura that we are speaking about and gathering for its sake is not a segment of time.

It is a **reality in which time, space, the event, the persons, and history have merged.** And the emotions, the conscience, and the doctrine have all merged in this title. Ashura is not an event for us and is not a segment of time that passed or whose likes will pass from the different segments of time. Ashura is a **comprehensive reality** in which all these meanings merge, from the doctrine, the conscience, the emotions, the feelings, the times, the places, and history. And the question says: What is Ashura?
Segment 2: The Philosophy of Quiddity and the Strategic Project

Sheikh al-Ghizzi delves into the philosophical definition of Ashura. He explains the concept of "quiddity" (the essence of a thing) and applies it to Ashura. He describes Ashura as a "giant strategic project" with three levels of objectives: short, medium, and long. The short-term objective was to uncover the truth about the Ummah's deviation and "coup" after the Prophet's passing, tracing this deviation back to a secret document (the Sahifah) that predated the Saqifah.

The philosophers, when they speak about quiddities... **quiddity** as defined by the philosophers is that which falls in the answer to "what is it?". When the questioner asks, saying, "What is the mountain? What is the sea?", whatever answer is given to this question is said to be the quiddity of the thing.

Quiddities are the manifestations of the realities of things, and the discussion is always about quiddities because the human mind cannot grasp the realities, but rather it hovers around them. When the question comes, "What is Ashura?", the answer will be in two horizons.

The first horizon: Ashura is a **giant strategic project**, name it what you will, just as strategic projects are named. Ashura is a giant project. And giant projects set objectives for them in three ways: short-term objectives, medium-term objectives, and long-term objectives.

Where does Ashura fall here? Its short-term objectives. The **short-term objective of Ashura is to uncover the truth** in that segment of time in which Ashura occurred. The Ummah, after the Messenger of Allah, blessings of Allah be upon him and his family, turned back on its heels. The matter in our narrations and in our books is very clear, that the **Ummah staged a coup.**

The narrations are clear with us and with others that the Ummah turned back on its heels. Therefore, the Seal of the Prophets, as in the hadiths of Al-Bukhari and Muslim, says to his companions at the Basin of Kawthar, "Away with you, away with you. And distance, distance to you," when they are driven away and expelled from the Basin.

The Ummah staged a coup, and Ashura, this giant project, in its short-term objective, came to uncover the truth. The narration is in the noble Al-Kafi from Abu Basir from our Imam Al-Sadiq, blessings of Allah and His peace be upon him. And the Imam speaks about the conspiracy of the **Sahifah**, the document which was written by a group of the Prophet's companions and which produced the Saqifah after that. Imam Al-Sadiq says:
In the knowledge of Allah, if the document is written, Husayn is killed.

Meaning that the killing of Husayn is not from the day of Saqifah, but rather from the day of the Sahifah. **If the document is written, Husayn is killed.** The narration wants to point out that this document, which is the title of deviation, has no cure except by uncovering its threads, and its threads are not uncovered except by the killing of Husayn, peace be upon him.
Segment 3: The Origins of Shiism and the Objectives of Ashura

Sheikh al-Ghizzi concludes his explanation of the "short-term objective" of Ashura, which was to distinguish the believers by making the deviation of the Ummah crystal clear. He refutes the idea that Shiism originated after Ashura, arguing that Shiism is identical to the original Islam of the Prophet. He introduces the "medium-term objective," preserving the continuity of the Progeny through Imam al-Sajjad, and the "long-term objective": preparing the ground for the global Mahdawi project.

Therefore they said: If literature were a man, he would be a Shiite. This is the short-term objective of Ashura. Which is uncovering the truth—the coup of the Ummah. So the blood of Husayn, prayers of Allah and His peace be upon him, was the lamp that uncovered the places of weakness so that the believers are distinguished from others.

Before Ashura, there were no prominent features or clear signs in the rites that distinguished the Shia from others. Therefore, historians imagined that Shiism originated after Ashura. This does not mean that Shiism originated after Ashura. **Shiism is Islam.** Since the mission began and since Gabriel, peace be upon him, spoke his word "Read", Shiism was born with the word "Read". And therefore the narrations in the books of the people before our books, the Seal of the Prophets, he diagnoses this meaning:
This one [Ali] and his Shia are the victorious ones.

**Shiism is Islam and that Islam is Shiism.** And there is no historical separation between Islam and Shiism. A great mistake repeated by many Shia is to make the methodology of the Ahlul Bayt as if it is another sect added to the other sects. The methodology of the Ahlul Bayt is Islam, and there is no Islam outside this methodology.

Ashura in its short-term objective diagnoses this truth. There is a Husayn. And there are other things. There is a Husayn who is a clear, **bold milestone**. The **medium-term objective** of this giant project of Ashura is to **preserve the permanence of the Progeny** and the continuity of the Progeny. Therefore, you notice the Master of Martyrs in every moment, he returns to the tent of Al-Sajjad and follows up on his conditions. And the eyes of the Father of the Pure Progeny remained on the tent of his Sajjad until the last moment.

As for the **long-term objective** in Ashura, it is the Hujjah son of Al-Hasan, the **Mahdawi project**. This is the final fruit of Ashura, the project of the Imam of our time, blessings of Allah and His peace be upon him.
Segment 4: Reading Husayn and the Horizon of Essence

Sheikh al-Ghizzi explores the "Second Horizon" of understanding Ashura. He explains that to answer "What is Ashura?", one must first ask "What is Husayn?". He draws a strict distinction between asking "Who is Husayn?" (an impossible question about his infinite, divine essence) and "What is Husayn?" (a question about his manifestations and effects). He categorizes the various ways people have historically studied Husayn, introducing his own method: the "Conscious Reading" (Al-Qira'ah Al-Wa'iyah) which focuses on effects rather than essence.

The second horizon by which I answer this question: What is Ashura? I say: **What is Husayn?** Ashura is a manifestation of a reality whose name is Husayn. I ask here: What is Husayn? And I did not ask: **"Who is Husayn?"** For I do not dare to do that. "Who is Husayn?" is a question that needs an answer that encompasses the boundaries. Is there anyone among us who can encompass the boundaries of Husayn?

But when I say: **What is Husayn?** The answer will be about his effects, not about his essence. When I say: Who is Husayn? The answer is about his essence. Husayn is the divine reality that has manifested in all aspects of this existence. So I do not answer this question: Who is Husayn? But I answer this question: What is Husayn?

If we want to answer this question, we must read Husayn. How do we read Husayn? There is the **historical reading**. There is the **merits-based reading**. There is the **calamity-based reading**—reading Husayn as a recurring calamity. And there is the **analytical reading**—reading Husayn through the lens of sociology, politics, psychology, and jurisprudence.

All these readings we need if we want to know Ashura. But there is a fifth reading through which and by means of which I want to read Husayn: I call it and term it the **conscious reading**. And a conscious ear will consciously retain it.
Segment 5: The Methodology of Conscious Reading
